  <bead id="fizeau-361f4f4b">
    <title>routing: complete reliability — succeed against any available provider, fail only when no providers remain</title>
    <description>
GOAL
Fizeau's auto-router must reliably select an executable route whenever ANY configured provider is reachable and within quota. Today the drain stalls or routes to dead hosts even when subscription/cloud providers are healthy. Caller (DDx) should receive provider_connectivity / no_viable_provider ONLY when literally every configured provider is unavailable.

OBSERVED FAILURE MODES (from a 27-bead harness-interface refactor drain on 2026-05-14)
1. Policy-scoring disabled under bounds. When --min-power or --max-power is set, internal/routing/score.go:60 and :108 skip the policy-aware preference block entirely. A free subscription candidate at the floor power loses to a metered candidate at higher power purely due to the absent policy bonus. Tracked in child bead fizeau-dc3cf359.

2. Dead-host route loop without cross-provider escalation. When the smart-routing scorer ranks fiz/bragi/qwen3.5-27b above claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 (because the default-policy local-deployment bias is winning), and bragi is unreachable, the request fails after exhausting bragi's ports (1234, 8020) and grendel's ports (1234, 1235, 8000) within a single bead-attempt, then errors with provider_connectivity — even though claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 is sitting at score 186 in fiz route-status. Cross-provider escalation needs to fall through to subscription/cloud once local providers fail.

3. Cross-process route-health amnesia. Each new ddx-try process spawns a fresh in-process routehealth.Store. Bragi being unreachable was learned by the previous attempt and persisted into the bead's work-failed-routes (via fizeau-8134d6db + ddx-71629938), but the *next* bead's first dial still goes to bragi for 5s before falling back. Aggregate cost: ~10–20s of dial timeouts per fresh ddx-try. Tracked separately in fizeau-7cd66e6b (pending ADR-012 carve-out decision).

4. Score for the same candidate varies wildly between bounded and unbounded queries. fiz route-status shows claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 at score 186 (no bounds, default policy); the same candidate scores ~110 with --min-power 8. That delta is the missing policy bonus. Tracked in fizeau-dc3cf359.

CONTRACT (what 'reliable' means)
- Given a healthy provider configured in fizeau.config.yaml — local OR subscription OR openrouter — and a request without an explicit provider pin, the router MUST select a route that succeeds. Provider liveness is determined by route-health signal + first-attempt dial.
- Caller-supplied --harness, --min-power, --max-power constraints MUST NOT cause systematic preference for more-expensive in-bounds candidates over cheaper-but-equally-eligible ones.
- Once a provider has been observed unreachable in the current ddx-try attempt's per-request route exclusion list, the router MUST NOT re-attempt that provider until the exclusion expires or the worker is restarted.
- If literally every configured provider is unreachable AND every subscription provider has zero quota AND no cloud route is reachable, the router emits no_viable_provider and the caller can decide.
- The router MUST emit a clear routing-decision evidence event naming each candidate scored, why it was selected or rejected, and the final decision — so operators can audit 'why did it pick X over Y'.

SCOPE
- Fix the policy-scoring-under-bounds bug (fizeau-dc3cf359) — surgical change to internal/routing/score.go.
- Add an integration test: with bragi unreachable (mock dial-failing endpoint), a smart-policy request lands on claude (subscription) within one routing decision, no per-bead 30s timeout.
- Add an integration test: with --min-power 8, default-policy request picks the free-subscription candidate over the metered candidate at the same power.
- Add an integration test: with all configured providers dial-failing, the router returns no_viable_provider in under 2× per-attempt dial timeout (not 5× or more).
- Document the routing contract in docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003 §Routing (or a new section) so callers can rely on it.

NON-SCOPE
- Route-health cross-process persistence (fizeau-7cd66e6b is its own bead; depends on ADR-012 carve-out decision).
- Cooldown policy changes (ddx-side bead ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Connect-timeout tuning (fizeau-b5757624 already shipped).
- Provider config schema changes.

DEPS
- Decompose into child beads if scope exceeds 5 ACs / 500 lines / 5 unrelated files. Otherwise treat as one focused fix touching internal/routing/score.go + service_routing.go projection + tests.

CHILDREN
- fizeau-dc3cf359 (policy-scoring-under-bounds; surgical)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. The router selects a healthy subscription/cloud route when configured local providers are all unreachable within one routing decision (no per-bead-attempt 30s timeout). Integration test: TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription.
2. With --min-power 8 set, a free subscription candidate at power 8 outscores a metered candidate at power 10 of the same harness family under the default policy. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MinPowerPreservesSubscriptionPreference.
3. With --max-power 8 set, candidates above power 8 are not just penalized weakly (current (power-max)*1) but visibly excluded from selection when an in-bounds candidate exists. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerExcludesOverpoweredCandidates.
4. When literally every configured provider's first-dial check fails AND no subscription route has quota, the router returns no_viable_provider within (number_of_providers × 5s) — not multiplied. Integration test: TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast.
5. RouteDecision evidence (per service_routing.go RouteDecision shape) includes a per-candidate scoring breakdown with the components map from scoreComponents() so operators can audit any single route decision.
6. Existing routing tests in internal/routing/ and service_routing_test.go all green: go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the auto-router reliability fix and points at this bead.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, kind:reliability, spec:CONTRACT-003</labels>

  <bead id="fizeau-b5757624">
    <title>routing: bound TCP dial timeout to ~5s, not Go default ~30s</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
A dead local provider endpoint costs ~30 seconds of wall-clock per call before Fizeau surfaces the dial error. The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go's default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with "dial tcp ... i/o timeout". With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db's letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).

ROOT CAUSE
internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.

What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport's DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client's transport. Go's net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel's full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.

PROPOSED FIX
- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.
- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.
- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.
- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).
- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.
- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.

DEPS
None.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutDefault5s asserts that the openaicompat.NewClient default Transport has an explicit DialContext with timeout ≤5s.
2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.
3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)
4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
5. CHANGELOG.md notes the new ConnectTimeout default + tunable.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:provider, kind:bug</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="adef1f122716cc1e66e4eeb839e1c5e3fd6846a0" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T213814-ca66981b"/>

  <bead id="fizeau-8134d6db">
    <title>routing: honor caller exclusions and fast-fail on transport timeouts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Fizeau wastes 2m45s per call retrying a dead local endpoint (4 attempts with 1s/2s/4s/8s backoff + per-attempt 30s dial timeout), then surfaces a provider_connectivity error to the caller. Even when the caller (DDx) records the failed (provider, model) and asks for re-routing on the next attempt, Fizeau re-routes to the same dead endpoint because the caller has no way to express "exclude this (provider, model)" in the request, and routing decisions are made in isolation from caller-side health signals. Observed reproduction: with bragi:1234/qwen3.5-27b unreachable, a DDx drain of 12 beads attempted each bead, every bead routed to bragi/qwen3.5-27b, every bead spent ~2m45s in Fizeau retries, every bead failed identically. Net drain time ~33 minutes, zero beads completed.

ROOT CAUSE
Two contributing behaviors in internal/provider/* and internal/serviceimpl/*:
1. Connect-time errors (dial tcp ECONNREFUSED / ETIMEDOUT to a local IP) are treated the same as in-flight provider errors and run through the full retry curve (4 attempts × ~30s = 2m45s). A local-network dial that misses the SYN window will not succeed on subsequent attempts; the retry burns wall-clock for no value.
2. The route decision in internal/routehealth and the public RouteRequest type carry no caller-supplied exclusion list. Routes recorded as failed by the caller cannot be expressed to Fizeau. The internal routehealth Store tracks process-local feedback only — fine for within-call retries, but stateless across calls.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute to the public RouteRequest, where ExcludedRoute names (Provider, Model, optional Endpoint). The router must skip any candidate matching the list. Document in service.go and the routing API doc.
- For connect-time errors (errors.As to *net.OpError with Op=="dial" AND ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/EHOSTUNREACH), bypass the retry curve: 1 attempt, fail fast within ~5s. Reserve the retry curve for in-flight 5xx / partial-response errors that may transient retry usefully.
- When the request lists multiple viable endpoints for the same model (current or future endpoint-first config), on connect-failure to one endpoint, immediately try the next endpoint in the same call rather than surfacing failure. The order matches the existing route-decision strategy.
- Expose a routing-quality event recording "route excluded by caller hint" so observability can distinguish caller-driven re-routes from internal health signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign provider config syntax. ExcludedRoutes is additive; endpoint-list support can land separately.
- Do not change the existing routehealth in-process Store contract; only extend RouteRequest.
- Do not alter retry behavior for non-transport-class provider errors (5xx, rate limit, content-policy) in this bead.

DEPS
None at Fizeau; DDx-side bead to actually populate ExcludedRoutes from execute-loop-failed-routes is filed separately under ddx (ddx-8a7a6843 covers the DDx escalation-signaling side).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestRouteRequest_ExcludedRoutesSkipsCandidate asserts a candidate matching an entry in RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes is filtered out of the route decision.
2. TestRouteRequest_ExcludedRoutesAllowsOtherCandidates asserts non-matching candidates remain selectable.
3. TestProviderDialError_FailsFastBelow5Seconds asserts that a dial-tcp-refused / dial-tcp-timeout error returns from the provider call in under 5 seconds (no 4-attempt retry curve).
4. TestProviderInflightError_StillRetries asserts in-flight 5xx errors keep the existing retry behavior (regression guard).
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
6. CHANGELOG.md and docs reference the new RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes field and the connect-time fast-fail policy.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:routehealth, kind:bug, spec:endpoint-first-routing</labels>

  <bead id="fizeau-59697e9d">
    <title>Async refresh scheduler and harnessByName helper</title>
    <description>
Source manifest: .ddx/beads-harness-interface-refactor.yaml
Source bead ID: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-04
Governing plan: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md

See the manifest and the plan for full context, touch-point inventory, and sub-sequence ordering.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Service struct gains a harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness method consulting the registered-harnesses field
2. service_refresh_scheduler.go owns a debounced ticker per registered QuotaHarness firing at QuotaFreshness()/2 and per AccountHarness whose AccountFreshness() differs from quota
3. Scheduler discovers harnesses by iterating service.harnessByName over the registry and type-asserting to QuotaHarness / AccountHarness independently
4. Scheduler reads QuotaStatus before each potential refresh to obtain CapturedAt; on cache-cold (State=QuotaUnavailable) the scheduler always calls RefreshQuota
5. Scheduler skips a tick when last-known QuotaStatus.CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now (cache-freshness dedup is the scheduler's job, not RefreshQuota's)
6. Startup refresh kickoff fires once on scheduler construction for every registered QuotaHarness / AccountHarness with a cache-cold state, per the cache-cold path in primary-harness-capability-baseline.md
7. Fake-clock tests prove debounce cadence, single-flight respect via cache lock, startup kickoff, and clean ctx cancel
8. Per-harness Refresh*Async helpers remain in place for now (deleted in each harness's e-step)
    </acceptance>

  <bead id="fizeau-59697e9d">
    <title>Async refresh scheduler and harnessByName helper</title>
    <description>
Source manifest: .ddx/beads-harness-interface-refactor.yaml
Source bead ID: BEAD-HARNESS-IF-04
Governing plan: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md

See the manifest and the plan for full context, touch-point inventory, and sub-sequence ordering.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Service struct gains a harnessByName(name string) harnesses.Harness method consulting the registered-harnesses field
2. service_refresh_scheduler.go owns a debounced ticker per registered QuotaHarness firing at QuotaFreshness()/2 and per AccountHarness whose AccountFreshness() differs from quota
3. Scheduler discovers harnesses by iterating service.harnessByName over the registry and type-asserting to QuotaHarness / AccountHarness independently
4. Scheduler reads QuotaStatus before each potential refresh to obtain CapturedAt; on cache-cold (State=QuotaUnavailable) the scheduler always calls RefreshQuota
5. Scheduler skips a tick when last-known QuotaStatus.CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now (cache-freshness dedup is the scheduler's job, not RefreshQuota's)
6. Startup refresh kickoff fires once on scheduler construction for every registered QuotaHarness / AccountHarness with a cache-cold state, per the cache-cold path in primary-harness-capability-baseline.md
7. Fake-clock tests prove debounce cadence, single-flight respect via cache lock, startup kickoff, and clean ctx cancel
8. Per-harness Refresh*Async helpers remain in place for now (deleted in each harness's e-step)
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:harness, kind:refactor, spec:CONTRACT-004</labels>

  <bead id="fizeau-b5757624">
    <title>routing: bound TCP dial timeout to ~5s, not Go default ~30s</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
A dead local provider endpoint costs ~30 seconds of wall-clock per call before Fizeau surfaces the dial error. The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go's default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with "dial tcp ... i/o timeout". With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db's letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).

ROOT CAUSE
internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.

What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport's DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client's transport. Go's net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel's full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.

PROPOSED FIX
- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.
- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.
- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.
- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).
- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.
- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.

DEPS
None.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutDefault5s asserts that the openaicompat.NewClient default Transport has an explicit DialContext with timeout ≤5s.
2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.
3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)
4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
5. CHANGELOG.md notes the new ConnectTimeout default + tunable.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:provider, kind:bug</labels>

.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T211849-453a3786/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent
owns provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.

- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T230343-e6f97d11/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T230343-e6f97d11/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T230343-e6f97d11/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T230343-e6f97d11/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T002844-5047397d/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T002844-5047397d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T002844-5047397d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/embedded/agent-codex-1778828615829215511.jsonl:12:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":11,"time":"2026-05-15T07:04:07.010595643Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T070324-3cfef09c","base_rev":"9ab2747a0d88f7d6a33e2e4ac50c4b9fff4b6f01","bead_id":"fizeau-3ff9878f","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T070324-3cfef09c/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"9ccfb03bdba32a59164f20e460f3259bb442f3bf365a6aa19bc20267eaecf082","session_id":"eb-eba0fab6","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_6","output":"# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close\n\n## Mode note\n\nThis reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as\n`ddx work`, `ddx try \u003cid\u003e`, \"execute bead `\u003cid\u003e`\", or \"start the worker\".\n\nIf the user said \"what should I work on next?\", \"what's blocking the queue?\", or\n\"work the queue\" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in\n**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward\nplans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in\nresponse to broad orientation prompts.\n\n---\n\n\"Doing work\" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,\nrun one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on\nsuccess or leave it available for a future eligible retry.\n\n## Default surface: `ddx work`\n\n```bash\nddx work\n```\n\n`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.\n`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx\nowns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent\nowns provider/model routing.\n\nFlags worth knowing:\n\n- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).\n- `--poll-interval \u003cdur\u003e` — continuous worker mode; wait between\n  iterations.\n- `--min-power \u003cn\u003e` / `--max-power \u003cn\u003e` — requested agent power bounds.\n- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.\n- `--harness \u003cname\u003e` / `--provider \u003cname\u003e` / `--model \u003cref\u003e` — passthrough\n  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.\n\n## Primitive: `ddx try`\n\nFor targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:\n\n```bash\nddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e\nddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e --from \u003crev\u003e      # base commit override\nddx try \u003cbead-id\u003e --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land\n```\n\n`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`\nfor the actual agent invocation.\n\n## Pick by default, not by ID\n\nUnder normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks\nthe top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only\npin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try \u003cid\u003e`) or\nwhen the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to\noverride.\n\n## Verify independently before closing\n\n**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the\nagent's self-report. Before closing a bead:\n\n1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's\n   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.\n2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from\n   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the\n   attempt.\n3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?\n   (`[ddx-\u003cid\u003e]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)\n\nIf all three pass: close the bead.\n\n## Close on success, unclaim on failure\n\nDDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome\nmaps to a concrete follow-up action:\n\n| Outcome | Meaning | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close \u003cid\u003e` |\n| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close \u003cid\u003e` |\n| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |\n| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |\n| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |\n| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |\n| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |\n| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |\n\n`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running\n`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e\n--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another\nworker can pick it up.\n\n**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or\nunclaims.\n\n## Testing expectations for bead implementations\n\nWhen an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests\nthat exercise the new code:\n\n- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party\n  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call\n  sequences test implementation, not behavior.\n- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost\n  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real\n  HTTP via a local test server).\n- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock\n  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their\n  scope.\n- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't\n  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the\n  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.\n- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC\n  mentions \"faster\" or \"scales\", the acceptance test must include:\n  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's\n  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.\n\n## Anti-patterns\n\n- **Trusting the agent's \"done\"**: always re-run the AC command\n  yourself before closing.\n- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`\n  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`\n  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.\n- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is\n  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only\n  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.\n- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the\n  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or\n  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled\n  tests.\n- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker\n  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat\n  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.\n\n## CLI reference\n\n```bash\nddx work                                    # default queue drain\nddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop\nddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker\nddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts\nddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint\n\nddx try \u003cid\u003e                                # one bead attempt\nddx try \u003cid\u003e --from \u003crev\u003e                   # override base commit\nddx try \u003cid\u003e --no-merge                     # preserve iteration\n```\n\nFull flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.\n"}}

.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T002518-ff7f26d4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T225643-dc61e0aa/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

CONTRACT-003 amended to document the new state, error, and operator config knobs.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T05:50:17Z","closing_commit_sha":"e01e404a7623abca214bb6f8586cae5ebcf4d1c8","created_at":"2026-05-02T18:05:43.402325681Z","description":"Auto-routing today handles per-request transient feedback (RecordRouteAttempt; CONTRACT-003 line 1413) but does NOT track per-provider quota state across requests. Result: when a provider hits its subscription/daily cap, every subsequent dispatch fails until operator notices. The 'point of auto-routing' is exactly this: take a quota-exhausted provider out of rotation, keep dispatching to others, periodically recheck.\n\n## Multi-account model (downstream operator decision, locked)\n\nEach API key = its own provider entry in ~/.config/agent/config.yaml (e.g., 'claude-acct-1', 'claude-acct-2'). Fizeau treats them independently. No new schema for multi-key-per-provider; reuse existing provider abstraction. Operator-friendly: rotate accounts by adding entries.\n\n## Prediction model (locked)\n\nCombine two signals:\n1. Provider-supplied headers when available (Anthropic X-RateLimit-Remaining-* family, OpenAI rate-limit headers, OpenRouter equivalents)\n2. Local burn-rate tracking — Fizeau maintains rolling per-provider token-usage window, compares against operator-configured per-provider budget (e.g., providers.claude-acct-1.daily_token_budget: 1_000_000)\n\nWhen EITHER signal predicts exhaustion before the next reset, take the provider out proactively (don't wait for the actual quota error).\n\n## Behavior on take-out\n\n- Provider transitions to 'quota_exhausted' state with an estimated retry_after timestamp (from headers when available; from window-reset estimate from local tracking otherwise)\n- Routing engine excludes quota_exhausted providers from candidate selection\n- Other providers continue serving requests normally\n- Periodic probe (lightweight HealthCheck) at provider's retry_after OR at a fallback interval (default 5 min) returns the provider to 'available' on success\n\n## New error class: NoViableProviderForNow\n\nDistinct from existing 'no-candidate' / 'config' errors. Returned when Execute has zero eligible providers solely because all are quota_exhausted (i.e., would otherwise be available). Includes:\n  - retry_after timestamp (earliest expected provider recovery)\n  - exhausted_providers []string (which providers are out)\n\nDDx (downstream) consumes this to pause its drain loop and resume on retry_after.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Rate-limit handling (per-second / per-minute throttle via 429 + Retry-After header) — this stays in the per-request feedback path; provider stays in rotation. DDx handles per-bead retry. Distinct from this bead.\n- Auto-account-creation / billing integration. Operator manages keys.\n- Cost-budget enforcement (different concern; lives in DDx --max-cost flag)","events_attachment":"agent-63bf58a6/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"agent-63bf58a6","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:fizeau","area:routing","kind:feature","downstream:ddx"],"notes":"Decomposed into children: fizeau-92b4b823 (state machine + NoViableProviderForNow error), fizeau-50b14297 (rate-limit header parsing for Anthropic/OpenAI/OpenRouter), fizeau-f2661619 (burn-rate tracker + daily_token_budget config), .execute-bead-wt-agent-63bf58a6-20260502T192505-092f5e0d-5208511b (periodic HealthCheck probe), .execute-bead-wt-agent-63bf58a6-20260502T192505-092f5e0d-78f47557 (CONTRACT-003 amendment). .ddx/executions/20260514T213955-5b3467be/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213955-5b3467be/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213955-5b3467be/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T213955-5b3467be/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.

The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go\u0026#39;s default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with \u0026#34;dial tcp ... i/o timeout\u0026#34;. With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db\u0026#39;s letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;ROOT CAUSE\u0026#xA;internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport\u0026#39;s DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client\u0026#39;s transport. Go\u0026#39;s net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel\u0026#39;s full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PROPOSED FIX\u0026#xA;- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.\u0026#xA;- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.\u0026#xA;- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.\u0026#xA;- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;NON-SCOPE\u0026#xA;- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).\u0026#xA;- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.\u0026#xA;- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;DEPS\u0026#xA;None.\n    \u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003cacceptance\u003e\n1. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutDefault5s asserts that the openaicompat.NewClient default Transport has an explicit DialContext with timeout ≤5s.\u0026#xA;2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.\u0026#xA;3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)\u0026#xA;4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.\u0026#xA;5. The fizeau-8134d6db AC required dial-class failures to bail in under 5 seconds; the shipped implementation only short-circuits the *retry loop* (one attempt instead of four), but the *dial itself* still uses Go\u0026#39;s default TCP connect timeout (~30s on Linux). Observed today: every bead routed to bragi:1234 / bragi:8020 / grendel:1234 / grendel:1235 / grendel:8000 took ~30s to fail with \u0026#34;dial tcp ... i/o timeout\u0026#34;. With 14 beads in the ddx queue all routing to a single dead host, the drain spent ~7 minutes establishing that the host was dead — meeting fizeau-8134d6db\u0026#39;s letter (single attempt) while missing its spirit (under 5s).\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;ROOT CAUSE\u0026#xA;internal/core/loop.go:643 (IsDialError short-circuit) is correctly wired. The retry-loop layer fails fast.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;What is NOT bounded: the underlying http.Transport\u0026#39;s DialContext. internal/provider/openai/openai.go and internal/sdk/openaicompat construct openaicompat.Config without overriding the HTTP client\u0026#39;s transport. Go\u0026#39;s net/http default Transport has no explicit DialContext, so it uses net.Dialer{} with Timeout=0 — which on Linux waits for the kernel\u0026#39;s full TCP SYN retransmit timeout (≈21s + RTO backoff = 30s observed). For a known-local endpoint that the operator just unplugged, 30s is pure waste.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PROPOSED FIX\u0026#xA;- Configure an explicit net.Dialer with Timeout=5s (or ServiceOptions-driven, defaulting to 5s) in the openaicompat client construction path.\u0026#xA;- Wrap http.Transport.DialContext so the connect phase is bounded independent of the request streaming timeout.\u0026#xA;- Keep the request-streaming timeout (Timeout field) separate — large requests legitimately take minutes once connected.\u0026#xA;- Expose ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config so callers (DDx, benchmarks) can tune for high-latency networks if needed; default 5s.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;NON-SCOPE\u0026#xA;- Do not change the retry-loop short-circuit (already correct).\u0026#xA;- Do not change in-flight 5xx retry behavior.\u0026#xA;- Do not bound the request body / streaming timeout in this bead.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;DEPS\u0026#xA;None.\n    \u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003cacceptance\u003e\n1. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutDefault5s asserts that the openaicompat.NewClient default Transport has an explicit DialContext with timeout ≤5s.\u0026#xA;2. TestOpenAIClient_DialTimeoutConfigurable asserts ConnectTimeout in openaicompat.Config overrides the default.\u0026#xA;3. TestProviderDeadLocalHost_FailsUnder5s — integration test: aim at an unroutable RFC 5737 address (e.g., 192.0.2.1:1) and assert the provider call returns within 5.5s with a dial error. (No process listening means kernel-level connect timeout exercises the bounded dial.)\u0026#xA;4. go test ./... -count=1 passes.\u0026#xA;5. CHANGELOG.md notes the new ConnectTimeout default + tunable.\n    \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n    \u003clabels\u003earea:routing, area:provider, kind:bug\u003c/labels\u003e\n    \u003cmetadata base-rev=\"adef1f122716cc1e66e4eeb839e1c5e3fd6846a0\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/20260514T213814-ca66981b\"/\u003e\n  \u003c/bead\u003e\n  \u003cgoverning\u003e\n    \u003cnote\u003eNo governing references were pre-resolved. .ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T213707-613466dc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T224436-12f97179/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T224436-12f97179/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T224436-12f97179/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T224436-12f97179/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T224436-12f97179/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
