Used by tests that verify the right tools\\n   406\\t// land at the right harness given the request's permission level.\\n   407\\ttype ToolWiringHook func(harness string, toolNames []string)\\n   408\\t```\\n   409\\t\\n   410\\t## Reasoning contract\\n   411\\t\\n   412\\t`Reasoning` is the only preferred public control for model-side reasoning.\\n   413\\tConsumers do not set separate public thinking, effort, level, or budget fields.\\n   414\\tThe scalar accepts named values (`auto`, `off`, `low`, `medium`, `high`) and\\n   415\\tprovider/harness-supported extended values such as `minimal`, `xhigh` /\\n   416\\t`x-high`, and `max`. It also accepts numeric values through\\n   417\\t`ReasoningTokens(n)`, where `0` means explicit off and positive integers mean\\n   418\\tan explicit max reasoning-token budget or documented provider-equivalent\\n   419\\tnumeric value.\\n   420\\t\\n   421\\tNormalization is tri-state:\\n   422\\t\\n   423\\t- Empty means no caller preference.\\n   424\\t- `auto` means resolve model, catalog, or provider defaults.\\n   425\\t- `off`, `none`, `false`, and `0` mean explicit reasoning off.\\n   426\\t- Positive integers mean an explicit numeric request.\\n   427\\t\\n   428\\tDefault portable named-to-token budgets are `low=2048`, `medium=8192`, and\\n   429\\t`high=32768` only when a selected provider/model does not publish a more\\n   430\\tspecific map. Providers and subprocess harnesses may map resolved reasoning to\\n   431\\twire or CLI knobs named `reasoning`, `thinking`, `effort`, `variant`, or a\\n   432\\tnumeric budget. They may also drop auto/default reasoning controls for models\\n   433\\tthat do not support explicit reasoning control. Explicit unsupported values,\\n   434\\tunknown extended values, and over-limit numeric values fail clearly.\\n   435\\t\\n   436\\tCatalog tier defaults are part of route resolution: below-smart tiers\\n   437\\t(`cheap`, `fast`, `standard`, `code-economy`, and `code-medium`) default to\\n   438\\t`reasoning=off`, including local/economy Qwen targets. `smart` and `code-high`\\n   439\\tdefault to `reasoning=high`. Any explicit caller `Reasoning` value wins over\\n   440\\tthese defaults, including supported values above high such as `xhigh` or\\n   441\\t`max`, and numeric values.\\n   442\\t\\n   443\\t## Sampling contract\\n   444\\t\\n   445\\t`ExecuteRequest.Temperature` and `ExecuteRequest.Seed` are the portable\\n   446\\tsampling controls. `Temperature=0` requests deterministic sampling. `Seed=0`\\n   447\\tmeans unset and lets the provider choose. Native OpenAI-compatible providers\\n   448\\thonor both fields. Providers or subprocess harnesses that do not expose an\\n   449\\tequivalent seed control may ignore `Seed`; callers that require strict parity\\n   450\\tmust treat those runs as advisory/non-deterministic.\\n   451\\t\\n   452\\t## Bead Execution Policy\\n   453\\t\\n   454\\tDDx bead implementation should use a two-pass policy against this service:\\n   455\\ttry a cheap or standard profile with `reasoning=off` first, then escalate only\\n   456\\twhen the first pass produced evidence that a smarter model is likely to help.\\n   457\\tThe initial pass should use `ModelRef=cheap` or `ModelRef=standard` with an\\n   458\\texplicit `ReasoningOff` request so local/economy models do not spend\\n   459\\treasoning tokens by default.\\n   460\\t\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_41\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'git branch --all --contains v0.5.0''^{}'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_41\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc 'git branch --all --contains v0.5.0''^{}'\",\"aggregated_output\":\"* master\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_42\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"git show HEAD:service_execute.go | nl -ba | sed -n '62,78p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\n{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_42\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"git show HEAD:service_execute.go | nl -ba | sed -n '62,78p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"    62\\t//   - A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across\n16\tsix service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the\n17\tcontract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25\n18\tsymbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.\n19\t\n20\tCONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every\n21\texisting harness, every service consumer, every test, and the\n22\tpublic-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces\n23\tthe boundary with a lint rule.\n24\t\n25\t## Requirements\n26\t\n27\t### Functional\n28\t\n29\t- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it\n30\t  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,\n31\t  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),\n32\t  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).\n33\t- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,\n34\t  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in\n35\t  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,\n36\t  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner\n37\t  constructor used by the dispatcher.\n38\t- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,\n39\t  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,\n40\t  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,\n41\t  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed\n42\t  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).\n43\t- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,\n44\t  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same\n45\t  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.\n46\t- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes\n47\t  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`\n48\t  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.\n49\t\n50\t### Non-Functional\n51\t\n52\t- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface\n53\t  early.\n54\t- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;\n55\t  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.\n56\t- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON\n57\t  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,\n58\t  not type-name encoding).\n59\t- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%\n60\t  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before\n61\t  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages\n62\t  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows\n63\t  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted\n64\t  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.\n65\t\n66\t## Touch-Point Inventory\n67\t\n68\tEvery file that changes, with the symbols being migrated.\n69\t\n70\t### Harness packages\n71\t\n72\t| Package | Changes |\n73\t|---------|---------|\n74\t| `internal/harnesses/types.go` | Add `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, `ModelDiscoveryHarness` interfaces; add `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`, `RoutingPreference`, `ErrAliasNotResolvable` sentinel. Every later migration step runs\n202\tagainst this suite.\n203\t\n204\t**Work**:\n205\t- New package `internal/harnesses/harnesstest/` (test helper, not\n206\t  test-only) containing:\n207\t  - `RunHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.Harness)` — asserts\n208\t    `Info`, `HealthCheck` (without invoking the binary), and the\n209\t    `Execute` setup-vs-runtime-error contract.\n210\t  - `RunQuotaHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.QuotaHarness)` —\n211\t    asserts `QuotaStatus` returns a valid `QuotaStatus` value (not\n212\t    an error) when the cache is empty, asserts `QuotaFreshness` is\n213\t    positive, reads `SupportedLimitIDs()` and asserts every emitted\n214\t    `Windows[].LimitID` (after driving a `RefreshQuota` against a\n215\t    cassette or synthetic state) is a member of that set. The suite\n216\t    asserts the **contract shape** of single-flight semantics —\n217\t    that concurrent `RefreshQuota` calls return without error and\n218\t    share a consistent post-state — but does not assert \"exactly one\n219\t    probe ran.\" Probe-count single-flight is testable on the\n220\t    synthetic harness (in-memory counter; covered in the synthetic\n221\t    test); on real harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) it requires\n222\t    binary-specific or filesystem-specific harnessing and lives in\n223\t    each harness's own package tests, not the shared suite.\n224\t  - `RunAccountHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.AccountHarness)` —\n225\t    analogous, including `AccountFreshness`.\n226\t  - `RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.ModelDiscoveryHarness)` —\n227\t    asserts non-empty `DefaultModelSnapshot`, reads\n228\t    `SupportedAliases()`, asserts `ResolveModelAlias` resolves each\n229\t    listed alias (positive path) and returns\n230\t    `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative\n231\t    path). Logically belongs with the\n278\t  scheduler; lifting it here means Step 5b can consume it without\n279\t  introducing it.\n280\t- New file `service_refresh_scheduler.go`. Owns:\n281\t  - A debounced ticker per registered `QuotaHarness` keyed by\n282\t    harness name, firing at `QuotaFreshness()/2` and calling\n283\t    `RefreshQuota(ctx)`.\n284\t  - Analogous tickers for `AccountHarness` instances whose\n285\t    freshness window differs from quota's (e.g. gemini's 7-day\n286\t    auth).\n287\t  - Startup refresh kickoff (cache-cold path from\n288\t    `primary-harness-capability-baseline.md`).\n289\t  - Tests with a fake clock that prove the scheduler debounces,\n290\t    respects single-flight (because `RefreshQuota` blocks on the\n291\t    harness's cache lock), and exits cleanly on context cancel.\n292\t- **Harness registration mechanism**: at construction time the\n293\t  scheduler iterates `service.harnessByName(name)` over every name\n294\t  in the existing harness registry and type-asserts each result to\n295\t  `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` independently. Pre-migration,\n296\t  type assertions fail for non-migrated harnesses and the scheduler\n297\t  registers nothing for them — they continue to use their existing\n298\t  `Refresh*Async` helpers. No\n300\t  explicit per-harness registration call is needed; the scheduler\n301\t  picks up new harnesses on its next construction.\n302\t- The scheduler is the only place that deduplicates probe calls by\n303\t  cache freshness; `QuotaHarness.RefreshQuota` itself probes\n304\t  unconditionally per CONTRACT-004. The scheduler skips a tick when\n305\t  the last-known `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` is within\n306\t  `QuotaFreshness()` of `now`.\n307\t- Per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions are not yet deleted — they\n308\t  stay until their owning harness migrates in Steps 5–8. Each\n309\t  harness's Step 5e/6e/7e sub-PR deletes its `Refresh*Async` after\n310\t  confirming the scheduler is driving its refresh cadence.\n311\t\n312\t**Size**: 1 PR.\n313\t\n314\t### Step 5 — Migrate claude harness (multi-PR sub-sequence)\n315\t\n316\t**Goal**: `claude.Runner` implements `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`,\n317\t`ModelDiscoveryHarness`. A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across\n16\tsix service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the\n17\tcontract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25\n18\tsymbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.\n19\t\n20\tCONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every\n21\texisting harness, every service consumer, every test, and the\n22\tpublic-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces\n23\tthe boundary with a lint rule.\n24\t\n25\t## Requirements\n26\t\n27\t### Functional\n28\t\n29\t- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it\n30\t  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,\n31\t  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),\n32\t  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).\n33\t- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,\n34\t  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in\n35\t  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,\n36\t  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner\n37\t  constructor used by the dispatcher.\n38\t- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,\n39\t  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,\n40\t  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,\n41\t  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed\n42\t  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).\n43\t- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,\n44\t  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same\n45\t  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.\n46\t- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes\n47\t  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`\n48\t  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.\n49\t\n50\t### Non-Functional\n51\t\n52\t- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface\n53\t  early.\n54\t- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;\n55\t  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.\n56\t- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON\n57\t  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,\n58\t  not type-name encoding).\n59\t- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%\n60\t  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before\n61\t  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages\n62\t  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows\n63\t  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted\n64\t  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.\n65\t\n66\t## Touch-Point Inventory\n67\t\n68\tEvery file that changes, with the symbols being migrated.\n69\t\n70\t### Harness packages\n71\t\n72\t| Package | Changes |\n73\t|---------|---------|\n74\t| `internal/harnesses/types.go` | Add `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`, `ModelDiscoveryHarness` interfaces; add `QuotaStatus`, `AccountSnapshot`, `QuotaStateValue`, `RoutingPreference`, `ErrAliasNotResolvable` sentinel. Keep existing `Harness` interface and existing public types untouched. |\n75\t| `internal/harnesses/claude/quota_cache.go` | Lowercase `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` → `claudeQuotaSnapshot`. Unexport `ReadClaudeQuota`, `ReadClaudeQuotaFrom`, `WriteClaudeQuota`, `ClaudeQuotaCachePath`, `IsClaudeQuotaFresh`, `ClaudeQuotaSnapshotAge`, `DefaultClaudeQuotaStaleAfter`. Delete `ClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision`, `DecideClaudeQuotaRouting`, `ReadClaudeQuotaRoutingDecision`. |\n76\t| `internal/harnesses/claude/runner.go` | Add `QuotaStatus`, `RefreshQuota`, `QuotaFreshness`, `AccountStatus`, `RefreshAccount`, `AccountFreshness`, `DefaultModelSnapshot`, `ResolveModelAlias` methods on `*Runner`. Internally call the now-unexported cache I/O. Acquire the harness's cache lock around refresh. |\n77\t| `internal/harnesses/claude/quota_pty.go` | Unexport `ReadClaudeQuotaViaPTY`, `RefreshClaudeQuotaViaPTY`, `ReadClaudeQuotaFromCassette`. Become internal helpers. |\n78\t| `internal/harnesses/claude/model_discovery.go` | Unexport `DefaultClaudeModelDiscovery`, `ResolveClaudeFamilyAlias`, `ReadClaudeReasoningFromHelp`. |\n79\t| `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go` | Mirror of claude: lowercase snapshot, unexport cache I/O, delete decision struct/func. |\n80\t| `internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go` | Add interface methods. Codex's session-token-count quota source folds into `RefreshQuota` as an internal fallback. |\n81\t| `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_pty.go` | Unexport. |\n82\t| `internal/harnesses/codex/session_token_count.go` | Unexport `ReadCodexQuotaFromSessionTokenCounts`, `CodexQuotaSnapshotFromTokenCountRateLimits`, `CodexSessionsRoot`. |\n83\t| `internal/harnesses/codex/account.go` | Unexport `ReadCodexAccount`, `ReadCodexAccountFrom`, `CodexAuthPath`. Every later migration step runs\n202\tagainst this suite.\n203\t\n204\t**Work**:\n205\t- New package `internal/harnesses/harnesstest/` (test helper, not\n206\t  test-only) containing:\n207\t  - `RunHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.Harness)` — asserts\n208\t    `Info`, `HealthCheck` (without invoking the binary), and the\n209\t    `Execute` setup-vs-runtime-error contract.\n210\t  - `RunQuotaHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.QuotaHarness)` —\n211\t    asserts `QuotaStatus` returns a valid `QuotaStatus` value (not\n212\t    an error) when the cache is empty, asserts `QuotaFreshness` is\n213\t    positive, reads `SupportedLimitIDs()` and asserts every emitted\n214\t    `Windows[].LimitID` (after driving a `RefreshQuota` against a\n215\t    cassette or synthetic state) is a member of that set. The suite\n216\t    asserts the **contract shape** of single-flight semantics —\n217\t    that concurrent `RefreshQuota` calls return without error and\n218\t    share a consistent post-state — but does not assert \"exactly one\n219\t    probe ran.\" Probe-count single-flight is testable on the\n220\t    synthetic harness (in-memory counter; covered in the synthetic\n221\t    test); on real harnesses (claude/codex/gemini) it requires\n222\t    binary-specific or filesystem-specific harnessing and lives in\n223\t    each harness's own package tests, not the shared suite.\n224\t  - `RunAccountHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.AccountHarness)` —\n225\t    analogous, including `AccountFreshness`.\n226\t  - `RunModelDiscoveryHarnessConformance(t, h harnesses.ModelDiscoveryHarness)` —\n227\t    asserts non-empty `DefaultModelSnapshot`, reads\n228\t    `SupportedAliases()`, asserts `ResolveModelAlias` resolves each\n229\t    listed alias (positive path) and returns\n230\t    `ErrAliasNotResolvable` for an out-of-set family (negative\n231\t    path). Empty `SupportedAliases()` skips the positive-path\n232\t    check (opencode/pi case).\n233\t  - `NewSyntheticQuotaHarness(name string, status QuotaStatus, limitIDs []string) QuotaHarness`,\n234\t    `NewSyntheticAccountHarness(name string, snapshot AccountSnapshot, freshness time.Duration) AccountHarness`,\n235\t    `NewSyntheticModelDiscoveryHarness(name string, snapshot ModelDiscoverySnapshot, aliases []string) ModelDiscoveryHarness` —\n236\t    in-memory implementations used by service-level tests to replace\n237\t    per-harness snapshot construction. Constructors accept the values\n238\t    each `Supported*` method should return so tests can exercise both\n239\t    populated and empty contract surfaces.\n240\t- Add a smoke test that runs the synthetic harnesses against the\n241\t  conformance suite (proves the suite works before any real harness\n242\t  consumes it).\n243\t\n244\t**Size**: 1 PR.\n245\t\n246\t### Step 3 — CONTRACT-003 projection helpers\n247\t\n248\t**Goal**: One projection helper per public type. Logically belongs with the\n278\t  scheduler; lifting it here means Step 5b can consume it without\n279\t  introducing it.\n280\t- New file `service_refresh_scheduler.go`. Owns:\n281\t  - A debounced ticker per registered `QuotaHarness` keyed by\n282\t    harness name, firing at `QuotaFreshness()/2` and calling\n283\t    `RefreshQuota(ctx)`.\n284\t  - Analogous tickers for `AccountHarness` instances whose\n285\t    freshness window differs from quota's (e.g. gemini's 7-day\n286\t    auth).\n287\t  - Startup refresh kickoff (cache-cold path from\n288\t    `primary-harness-capability-baseline.md`).\n289\t  - Tests with a fake clock that prove the scheduler debounces,\n290\t    respects single-flight (because `RefreshQuota` blocks on the\n291\t    harness's cache lock), and exits cleanly on context cancel.\n292\t- **Harness registration mechanism**: at construction time the\n293\t  scheduler iterates `service.harnessByName(name)` over every name\n294\t  in the existing harness registry and type-asserts each result to\n295\t  `QuotaHarness` and `AccountHarness` independently. Pre-migration,\n296\t  type assertions fail for non-migrated harnesses and the scheduler\n297\t  registers nothing for them — they continue to use their existing\n298\t  `Refresh*Async` helpers. No\n300\t  explicit per-harness registration call is needed; the scheduler\n301\t  picks up new harnesses on its next construction.\n302\t- The scheduler is the only place that deduplicates probe calls by\n303\t  cache freshness; `QuotaHarness.RefreshQuota` itself probes\n304\t  unconditionally per CONTRACT-004. The scheduler skips a tick when\n305\t  the last-known `QuotaStatus.CapturedAt` is within\n306\t  `QuotaFreshness()` of `now`.\n307\t- Per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions are not yet deleted — they\n308\t  stay until their owning harness migrates in Steps 5–8. Each\n309\t  harness's Step 5e/6e/7e sub-PR deletes its `Refresh*Async` after\n310\t  confirming the scheduler is driving its refresh cadence.\n311\t\n312\t**Size**: 1 PR.\n313\t\n314\t### Step 5 — Migrate claude harness (multi-PR sub-sequence)\n315\t\n316\t**Goal**: `claude.Runner` implements `QuotaHarness`, `AccountHarness`,\n317\t`ModelDiscoveryHarness`. .ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T231710-536bf9c7/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:116:{"acceptance":"DEFERRED — no implementation in this bead. Promoted from proposed
to open when one of the re-evaluation triggers fires.","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:44:35.860100935Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-c2a23daa","depends_on_id":"fizeau-7c8b1856","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:44:46Z"}],"description":"DEFERRED. Filed proposed-status for future work.

OpenRouter routes a single model (e.g. qwen/qwen3.6-27b) to multiple
upstream sub-providers (DeepInfra, Together, Fireworks, etc.). Each
has its own pricing, latency, context window, and rate limits. For
high-fidelity routing, the snapshot should distinguish them.

## Syntax (per ADR-012 naming convention)

`<provider>/<model>@<sub_provider>`. Examples:
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@deepinfra`
- `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b@together`
- `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6@anthropic`

## Source

OR's `/api/v1/models/<id>/endpoints` returns:
```
{
  "data": {
    "endpoints": [
      {"provider_name": "DeepInfra", "context_length": ..., ...},
      {"provider_name": "Together", "context_length": ..., ...}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Why deferred

- Per-OR-model API call (N+1 problem; OR has hundreds of models)
- Cardinality blowup in the snapshot (each model becomes N entries)
- Requires routing-layer changes to actually USE the sub-provider
  selection
- Marginal value for current benchmark-mode use case (we're not
  routing through OR at scale)

## Re-evaluate when

- We ship cost-based routing (ADR-011, bead E2) and quota signals
  start mattering across OR sub-providers
- We benchmark OR-Qwen3 across multiple upstream sub-providers and
  see measurable performance differences
- An operator explicitly requests it

## Depends on

`.ddx/executions/20260514T233141-852debdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

6. Old per-harness cache I/O functions still exported and unchanged; both surfaces coexist

.ddx/executions/20260515T052443-8b266248/embedded/agent-codex-1778822691604098816.jsonl:14:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":13,"time":"2026-05-15T05:25:27.183941481Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T052443-8b266248","base_rev":"1dda3fe508ef5dc15e421e30795f0afe51180e6f","bead_id":"fizeau-4530e0be","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T052443-8b266248","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T052443-8b266248/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"354e7a8d150f7a41cfc366a239cdda9d363463433031ff9465a99b744fb5f91f","session_id":"eb-4ae86db1","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_8","output":"# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent
owns provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.
"}}
.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T051415-4450bff6/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260515T061517-adc869f5/embedded/agent-codex-1778825725845403633.jsonl:12:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":11,"time":"2026-05-15T06:15:56.371307665Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T061517-adc869f5","base_rev":"32d95efceb2fd461cd7385dd3abe9f6018806226","bead_id":"fizeau-e2819b1a","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T061517-adc869f5","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T061517-adc869f5/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"85980722b69e4ab7bfbaa83a344ee30019535b6dd20ebe668f90f8d8c1d349fb","session_id":"eb-dae8962c","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_6","output":"# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent
owns provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.
"}}
.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

  AC5 — Existing tests still pass.

.ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/embedded/agent-codex-1778824941320898155.jsonl:12:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":11,"time":"2026-05-15T06:02:52.844309018Z","metadata":{"DDX_MODE":"bead_execution","attempt_id":"20260515T060210-10cfcac4","base_rev":"ae69f1c2e90ff6f1647c33538b10db33ebeb649a","bead_id":"fizeau-c5e4f20a","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260515T060210-10cfcac4/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"8ec5e85dbb7e36120f25874dfde763c37efc15688b17d0ed7fa303065a5769d3","session_id":"eb-ddbfcf22","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_6","output":"# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; the upstream agent
owns provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T062603-3643ecf5/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T004958-df3d7476/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
.ddx/attachments/agent-b5d67344/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"# no_changes_rationale — agent-b5d67344 (harness-parity: agent vs pi on omlx vidar qwen3.6)

## What is done in this pass

1. Verified vidar OMLX endpoint reachability from this Linux worktree:
   `curl http://vidar:1235/v1/models` returns the OMLX catalog and includes
   `Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit` (and the 35B-A3B variants). The native agent arm
   `agent-vidar-omlx-qwen36-27b` already exists in
   `scripts/beadbench/manifest-v1.json:32-39` with the correct provider/model pin.

2. Captured pi's reachable provider+model set on this machine:
     `pi --list-models` →
       providers: anthropic, google-gemini-cli, openai-codex, openrouter
       qwen3.6   : openrouter qwen/qwen3.6-plus           (NOT the omlx vidar
                                                           Qwen3.6-27B target)
       no entry for vidar / omlx / lmstudio / openai-compatible / local

3. Cross-checked pi's reviewer-model availability for the AC#1-pinned
   `--review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.5`:
     pi's openai-codex tier tops out at `gpt-5.4` (also `gpt-5.4-mini`,
     `gpt-5.3-codex`, etc.). `gpt-5.5` is not in pi's catalog.

## What is blocking

AC #2 ("Pi configuration verified: pi --list-models snapshot shows omlx is in
pi's reachable provider set") is **not satisfiable in this environment**. The
bead description anticipates this exact situation and lists three possible
unblockers:

  (a) Pi config-file edit on the test machine
        — explicitly out of scope per user directive
          "just consume what pi tells us"
  (b) A different test machine where pi already has omlx
        — execute-bead worktrees run on this Linux host; we cannot relocate
  (c) A pi command-line --provider flag pointing at vidar
        — pi has no openai-compatible / generic-http provider; the four
          providers listed above are the only options. There is no
          `--provider vidar-omlx` or equivalent.

Because pi cannot be made to call vidar OMLX, no `pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36` arm
can be exercised, no paired runs (AC #3, N≥8 in AC #4) can be produced,
no aggregate metrics (AC #5) or top-3 gaps ranked from pairwise data
(AC #6) can be filed honestly. Filing a one-arm "comparison" would defeat
the bead's purpose (driving the next iteration tweak from gap evidence).

A secondary blocker exists for the reviewer pin: `gpt-5.5` does not exist
in pi's catalog. Even if pi gained omlx, the manifest entry would have to
be reframed — likely against `gpt-5.4` (current codex top) or against the
codex CLI directly.

## What a follow-up attempt would need

To turn this bead green, one of the following must change first:

1. **Pi gains a generic openai-compatible provider** (e.g., `pi --provider
   openai-compat --base-url http://vidar:1235/v1 --model
   Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit`). Once `pi --list-models` (or a
   `--provider openai-compat` invocation) can reach vidar, AC #2 becomes
   satisfiable and the paired runs become possible.

   This likely requires either (a) a pi version bump that adds
   openai-compatible support, or (b) a pi config-file edit registering
   vidar as a custom provider — the latter is explicitly out of scope per
   the user directive cited in the bead.

2. **OR the bead is reframed** to compare the agent's vidar OMLX arm
   against pi's nearest-available qwen3.6 (openrouter
   `qwen/qwen3.6-plus`). That changes the comparison from "same model,
   different harness" to "different model + different harness", which
   confounds the iteration signal the bead wants. Worth flagging to the
   user, but a reframe — not the work this bead asked for.

3. **AND the reviewer pin should be updated** from `gpt-5.5` to a model
   that exists in pi's catalog (e.g., `gpt-5.4` or `gpt-5.4-mini`), or the
   reviewer should be the codex CLI directly rather than via pi.

## Why no partial commit

A partial commit that adds the agent arm + a placeholder pi arm + an
empty-table research doc would (a) leave the manifest in an inconsistent
state (AC #1 cites an unreachable arm), (b) seed
`docs/research/AR-2026-04-26-agent-vs-pi-omlx-vidar-qwen36.md` with no
real pairwise data (defeating AC #3-#6), and (c) hide the actual blocker
behind apparent progress, slowing the next iteration. Per execute-bead
guidance, "a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit" —
returning the bead with this rationale lets the user choose whether to
unblock pi or reframe the comparison before the next attempt is queued.

## Evidence captured (for the next attempt)

- vidar OMLX reachable: `curl http://vidar:1235/v1/models` → 200, model
  catalog includes `Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit`, `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`,
  `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-nvfp4`, `gpt-oss-20b-MXFP4-Q8`, etc.
- pi --list-models providers (full set): anthropic, google-gemini-cli,
  openai-codex, openrouter. No local / openai-compat / lmstudio / omlx.
- pi qwen3.6 catalog match: only `openrouter qwen/qwen3.6-plus`
  (1M ctx, 65.5K out, thinking=yes, images=yes) — not the local omlx
  target.
- pi codex top model: `openai-codex gpt-5.4` (272K ctx, 128K out,
  thinking=yes). `gpt-5.5` absent.
