 {"acceptance":"1. The router selects a healthy subscription/cloud route when configured local providers are all unreachable within one routing decision (no per-bead-attempt 30s timeout). Integration test: TestRouter_DeadLocalProvider_FallsThroughToSubscription.
2. With --min-power 8 set, a free subscription candidate at power 8 outscores a metered candidate at power 10 of the same harness family under the default policy. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MinPowerPreservesSubscriptionPreference.
3. With --max-power 8 set, candidates above power 8 are not just penalized weakly (current (power-max)*1) but visibly excluded from selection when an in-bounds candidate exists. Unit test: TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerExcludesOverpoweredCandidates.
4. When literally every configured provider's first-dial check fails AND no subscription route has quota, the router returns no_viable_provider within (number_of_providers × 5s) — not multiplied. Integration test: TestRouter_AllProvidersDead_FailsFast.
5. RouteDecision evidence (per service_routing.go RouteDecision shape) includes a per-candidate scoring breakdown with the components map from scoreComponents() so operators can audit any single route decision.
6. Existing routing tests in internal/routing/ and service_routing_test.go all green: go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the auto-router reliability fix and points at this bead.","created_at":"2026-05-14T23:29:53.368870466Z","description":"GOAL
Fizeau's auto-router must reliably select an executable route whenever ANY configured provider is reachable and within quota. Today the drain stalls or routes to dead hosts even when subscription/cloud providers are healthy. Caller (DDx) should receive provider_connectivity / no_viable_provider ONLY when literally every configured provider is unavailable.

OBSERVED FAILURE MODES (from a 27-bead harness-interface refactor drain on 2026-05-14)
1. Policy-scoring disabled under bounds. When --min-power or --max-power is set, internal/routing/score.go:60 and :108 skip the policy-aware preference block entirely. A free subscription candidate at the floor power loses to a metered candidate at higher power purely due to the absent policy bonus. Tracked in child bead fizeau-dc3cf359.

2. Dead-host route loop without cross-provider escalation. When the smart-routing scorer ranks fiz/bragi/qwen3.5-27b above claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 (because the default-policy local-deployment bias is winning), and bragi is unreachable, the request fails after exhausting bragi's ports (1234, 8020) and grendel's ports (1234, 1235, 8000) within a single bead-attempt, then errors with provider_connectivity — even though claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 is sitting at score 186 in fiz route-status. Cross-provider escalation needs to fall through to subscription/cloud once local providers fail.

3. Cross-process route-health amnesia. Each new ddx-try process spawns a fresh in-process routehealth.Store. Bragi being unreachable was learned by the previous attempt and persisted into the bead's work-failed-routes (via fizeau-8134d6db + ddx-71629938), but the *next* bead's first dial still goes to bragi for 5s before falling back. Aggregate cost: ~10–20s of dial timeouts per fresh ddx-try. Tracked separately in fizeau-7cd66e6b (pending ADR-012 carve-out decision).

4. Score for the same candidate varies wildly between bounded and unbounded queries. fiz route-status shows claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 at score 186 (no bounds, default policy); the same candidate scores ~110 with --min-power 8. That delta is the missing policy bonus. Tracked in fizeau-dc3cf359.

CONTRACT (what 'reliable' means)
- Given a healthy provider configured in fizeau.config.yaml — local OR subscription OR openrouter — and a request without an explicit provider pin, the router MUST select a route that succeeds. Provider liveness is determined by route-health signal + first-attempt dial.
- Caller-supplied --harness, --min-power, --max-power constraints MUST NOT cause systematic preference for more-expensive in-bounds candidates over cheaper-but-equally-eligible ones.
- Once a provider has been observed unreachable in the current ddx-try attempt's per-request route exclusion list, the router MUST NOT re-attempt that provider until the exclusion expires or the worker is restarted.
- If literally every configured provider is unreachable AND every subscription provider has zero quota AND no cloud route is reachable, the router emits no_viable_provider and the caller can decide.
- The router MUST emit a clear routing-decision evidence event naming each candidate scored, why it was selected or rejected, and the final decision — so operators can audit 'why did it pick X over Y'.

SCOPE
- Fix the policy-scoring-under-bounds bug (fizeau-dc3cf359) — surgical change to internal/routing/score.go.
- Add an integration test: with bragi unreachable (mock dial-failing endpoint), a smart-policy request lands on claude (subscription) within one routing decision, no per-bead 30s timeout.
- Add an integration test: with --min-power 8, default-policy request picks the free-subscription candidate over the metered candidate at the same power.
- Add an integration test: with all configured providers dial-failing, the router returns no_viable_provider in under 2× per-attempt dial timeout (not 5× or more).
- Document the routing contract in docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003 §Routing (or a new section) so callers can rely on it.

NON-SCOPE
- Route-health cross-process persistence (fizeau-7cd66e6b is its own bead; depends on ADR-012 carve-out decision).
- Cooldown policy changes (ddx-side bead ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Connect-timeout tuning (fizeau-b5757624 already shipped).
- Provider config schema changes.

DEPS
- Decompose into child beads if scope exceeds 5 ACs / 500 lines / 5 unrelated files. Otherwise treat as one focused fix touching internal/routing/score.go + service_routing.go projection + tests.

ROOT CAUSE
  CLI surface predates v0.11. --profile and --model-ref were the routing inputs; --min-power/--max-power were the only way to constrain power. With Profile→Policy renamed, ModelRef removed, and policies as the canonical surface, the CLI flags need a corresponding hard cut.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - New subcommands: `fiz policies` (modeled on `fiz providers`) and `fiz harnesses` (modeled on `fiz providers`). Both support text and --json output.
  - Drop --profile and --model-ref flags entirely (no deprecated aliases). Operators using those names get cobra "unknown flag" error.
  - Add --policy flag (canonical name). --min-power and --max-power stay (soft signals).
  - route-status JSON shape: rename "profile" → "policy" key; PowerPolicy.Profile → PowerPolicy.PolicyName.
  - Help text scrub: "profile" → "policy", "target" → drop or rename to "policy"/"model" depending on context, drop references to "model-ref".

  8. Help-text scrub: every CLI help string mentioning "profile", "target" (in routing context), or "model-ref" updated.

remaining, recent p50 latency) and write them to the runtime tier of
M1's cache. Consumed by M2's snapshot assembler at read time to set
KnownModel.Status, .QuotaRemaining, .RecentP50Latency.

## Public API sketch

```go
package runtimesignals

type Signal struct {
    Provider          string
    Status            modelregistry.ModelStatus
    QuotaRemaining    *int        // nil when unknown
    QuotaResetAt      *time.Time
    RecentP50Latency  time.Duration
    LastSuccessAt     *time.Time
    LastErrorAt       *time.Time
    LastErrorMsg      string
    RecordedAt        time.Time
}

func Collect(ctx context.Context, providerName string, cfg *config.ProviderConfig) (Signal, error)
```

## Sources per provider type

- **openrouter, openai-compat**: parse rate-limit headers from any
  recent response (already plumbed via commit 7776890e
  `quota_exhausted`); fetch `/usage` if available
- **claude-subscription**: existing `quota_cache.go` code in
  `internal/harnesses/claude/`; expose its data through this
  surface
- **codex-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go`
- **gemini-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go`
- **local providers (ds4, llama-server, omlx)**: status only (alive
  check via /v1/models or /props ping); no quota concept

## Aggregation

p50 latency comes from a sliding-window in-memory counter (recent N
requests per provider). On crash/restart, signal is reset (cache file
is empty until next collection). Acceptable — runtime signals are
short-TTL by design.

## Cache integration

Each provider gets its own `runtime/<provider>.json` file in M1's
cache. 5-minute TTL. Stale-while-revalidate: snapshot reads return
immediately, background refresh fires.

## In-scope files

- internal/runtimesignals/signal.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go — per-provider-type collectors
- internal/runtimesignals/headers.go — rate-limit header parsing
  (extends commit 7776890e)
- internal/runtimesignals/latency.go — sliding-window p50
- internal/runtimesignals/signal_test.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache infrastructure)
- Existing `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini}/quota_cache.go`
  (consumed, not modified — this bead exposes their data uniformly)

## Out of scope

- Snapshot assembly (M2).
- CLI (M4).
- Persistent historical metrics (out-of-scope per ADR-012; this is
  current-state only).","events_attachment":"fizeau-3e0f3737/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T01:38:53.823089565Z","id":"fizeau-3e0f3737","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:routing","area:cache","kind:feature"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5677bd4e","spec-id":"ADR-012","status":"closed","title":"Runtime signal collector: status, quota, recent latency → runtime cache","updated_at":"2026-05-12T01:56:10.8968959Z"}

```go
    RecordedAt        time.Time
}

func Collect(ctx context.Context, providerName string, cfg *config.ProviderConfig) (Signal, error)
```

## Sources per provider type

- **openrouter, openai-compat**: parse rate-limit headers from any
  recent response (already plumbed via commit 7776890e
  `quota_exhausted`); fetch `/usage` if available
- **claude-subscription**: existing `quota_cache.go` code in
  `internal/harnesses/claude/`; expose its data through this
  surface
- **codex-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go`
- **gemini-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go`
- **local providers (ds4, llama-server, omlx)**: status only (alive
  check via /v1/models or /props ping); no quota concept

## Aggregation

p50 latency comes from a sliding-window in-memory counter (recent N
requests per provider). On crash/restart, signal is reset (cache file
is empty until next collection). Acceptable — runtime signals are
short-TTL by design.

## Cache integration

Each provider gets its own `runtime/<provider>.json` file in M1's
cache. 5-minute TTL. Stale-while-revalidate: snapshot reads return
immediately, background refresh fires.

## In-scope files

- internal/runtimesignals/signal.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go — per-provider-type collectors
- internal/runtimesignals/headers.go — rate-limit header parsing
  (extends commit 7776890e)
- internal/runtimesignals/latency.go — sliding-window p50
- internal/runtimesignals/signal_test.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache infrastructure)
- Existing `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini}/quota_cache.go`
  (consumed, not modified — this bead exposes their data uniformly)

## Out of scope

- Snapshot assembly (M2).
- CLI (M4).
- Persistent historical metrics (out-of-scope per ADR-012; this is

All child beads are closed.\n4. go test ./... -count=1 passes after children land.","closing_commit_sha":"4eb0ec2f5ad44c3069860443b95b62664be4c961","created_at":"2026-05-13T17:47:27.527033106Z","description":"Follow-on epic for the approved routing plan from 2026-05-13.\n\nEnd state: every unpinned autorouting decision is a pure function of client inputs plus the current fiz models snapshot. The snapshot is the single source of routing facts: health, quota, model availability, context/tools/reasoning support, billing kind, effective cost, actual-cash-spend flag, locality, reliability, latency, utilization, and freshness. Route code must not maintain a second hidden provider-probe path.\n\nFreshness is a fiz contract, not a required fiz daemon. Fiz provides synchronous refresh APIs/CLI behavior with cross-process locks, single-flight/coalescing, TTLs, cooldowns, and atomic snapshot writes. Autorouting can synchronously refresh stale routing-relevant fields before scoring. CLI commands must not create refresh storms. fiz models is quick and reads the snapshot; if stale fields exist it prints clear refresh guidance rather than launching uncoordinated background probes. Foreground --refresh and --refresh-all are available for serverless operation.\n\nFiz also exposes an optional async refresh/warmup mechanism that an external long-lived owner such as ddx server can call on a heartbeat to maintain freshness. That async maintainer is an optimization only; route correctness must not depend on ddx server integration.\n\nRouting gates only on true constraints or dispatchability: pins, no_remote, metered actual-spend refusal, required tools/context/reasoning, known down endpoints, quota exhaustion, and pin conflicts. Everything else is scoring. Default view shows
the assembled snapshot from M2; flags filter and refresh. Detail view
via `fiz models <ref>` with shortform fuzzy resolution per ADR-012's
naming convention.

Also updates FEAT-006 (standalone CLI) doc to register the new
subcommand and document the interface.

## CLI surface

```
fiz models [flags]                     # list view
fiz models <ref> [flags]               # detail view
fiz cache prune                        # related cache GC
```

## Flags (list view)

- `--refresh`         Force-refresh all sources synchronously before showing
- `--no-refresh`      Don't trigger background refresh on stale data
- `--json`            Machine-readable output
- `--provider <name>` Filter to one provider
- `--power-min <n>`   Filter Power >= n
- `--power-max <n>`   Filter Power <= n
- `--include-noise`   Show low-power, unranked, deprecated entries
  (default suppresses to keep output clean)

## Default filter rules (suppress noise)

- Within (family, tier): keep top 2 versions (current + previous)
- Drop entries with Power < 3 (noise)
- Drop entries with Tier == TierUnknown AND Power == 0
- Show AutoRoutable=false but mark them clearly

`--include-noise` shows everything (useful for debugging the
snapshot, OR's long tail, etc.)

## Detail view (`fiz models <ref>`)

Resolution per ADR-012:
- Full canonical (`<provider>/<id>`) → exact lookup
- Shortform (no `/`) → search snapshot:
  - Exactly one match: use it
  - Multiple matches: list candidates with full canonical IDs, exit 1
  - No matches: show fuzzy-match suggestions, exit 1

Detail output: full KnownModel struct + raw discovery data + full
catalog entry + recent runtime signals + AutoRoutable composition
breakdown (which factor blocked, if any).

## Output format (default)

Tabular, columns: PROVIDER MODEL FAMILY VERSION TIER POWER COST/M
STATUS QUOTA AUTO

Color: AutoRoutable=true green, =false dim. Pre-release in italics.
Status colored (Available green, RateLimited yellow, Unreachable red).

`--json` emits the snapshot serialized as JSON.

## In-scope files

- cmd/fiz/models.go — subcommand registration
- cmd/fiz/models_list.go — list view + filters
- cmd/fiz/models_detail.go — detail view + resolution
- cmd/fiz/models_test.go
- cmd/fiz/cache_prune.go — `fiz cache prune` subcommand
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-standalone-cli.md — register
  `fiz models` and `fiz cache prune` subcommands; document flags

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M2 (snapshot assembler)
- M3 (runtime signals)

## Out of scope

- Routing's consumption of the snapshot (handled in routing-layer
  beads downstream; this bead is the operator UI)
- OR sub-provider syntax in resolution (M5 deferred)","events_attachment":"fizeau-491dd1e3/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T13:23:59.92698851Z","id":"fizeau-491dd1e3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:routing","kind:feature"],"notes":"Operator refined ACs into named test/build/doc checks and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.","owner":"erik","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-17d65f66","spec-id":"ADR-012","status":"closed","title":"fiz models top-level CLI subcommand with shortform fuzzy resolution","updated_at":"2026-05-12T13:53:46.852869553Z"}

5. `go test ./cmd/bench -count=1` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T00:43:23.806868136Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-4cf91f64","depends_on_id":"fizeau-9a179645","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T00:43:35Z"}],"description":"Problem
Adding a benchmark lane requires hand-editing unrelated YAML and shell blocks. The desired operator workflow is a single command that can clone an existing lane/profile, apply a few overrides, enroll it in recipes, and update short aliases.

In scope
- Add a local authoring command, preferably `fiz-bench lanes add` or `fiz-bench lanes clone`, rather than another standalone script if cmd/bench already owns the sweep schema.
- Command inputs should include source lane id, new lane id, new profile id, optional recipe ids, optional alias list, and key overrides such as provider/model/base_url/resource_group/quant_label/sampling/env.
- The command should update scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml and scripts/benchmark/profiles/<profile_id>.yaml using structured YAML APIs, preserving existing files as much as practical.
- Shell wrapper aliases should move to a data-driven block or generated helper so adding an alias is not another manual case arm.
- Include a dry-run mode that prints the planned file changes without writing.

5. `scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash` or an equivalent shellspec file defines the four test cases above against a mock HTTP server.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T06:14:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1864078","closing_commit_sha":"aeb009ff91eb5926da7513c1dceccb386328fcc9","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:45:58.814386643Z","description":"Add a per-lane preflight check to scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh that fires a single 1-token chat completion against each requested lane's endpoint before any matrix runner spawns. If the endpoint cannot produce a token within 30s using the lane's actual sampling params, fail the sweep with a non-zero exit and a clear message naming the lane, the endpoint, the model id, and the underlying error.

Why this matters: see parent epic. The 2026-05-10 sindri-llamacpp incident wasted 119 cells (~93 min) on a `reasoning: low` parameter the llama-server provider rejects. The same hour, sindri-vllm wasted multiple cells on a hung model engine where /v1/models responded but /v1/chat/completions never streamed tokens. A single curl-to-completion check per lane catches all these classes (plus auth failures, missing-model 404s, connection-refused, wrong content-type) at sweep startup, before any matrix runner spawns.

Behavior:

- Fire after `Resolved tasks:` is printed, before the per-lane "starting" markers.
- For each lane in the resolved plan: build a JSON body using the lane's `fizeau_env.FIZEAU_MODEL` and the lane's sampling block (temperature, top_p, top_k, reasoning when present). POST to `<base_url>/chat/completions` with `max_tokens: 1` and a 30s timeout.
- Pass condition: the response is JSON-parseable and `.choices[0].message.content` is a non-null string (even an empty string is OK — the model started decoding).
- Fail condition: any non-2xx, timeout, malformed JSON, or missing message content. Print the lane id, base_url, model id, sampling params attempted, and the response body (truncated to 1KB).
- Skip preflight for managed-cloud lanes that have always-on SLAs (openrouter, openai, anthropic) — these are well-known and a preflight just burns quota. Identify by checking the resource group's `provider_type` against an allow-list (vllm, llama-server, omlx, lmstudio, rapid-mlx, ollama, lucebox).

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — add preflight_lane() function + per-lane invocation loop right before the existing per-lane plan output.
- scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml — no schema change needed; the existing `fizeau_env` and `sampling` blocks are sufficient.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Pre-resume validation that the model server is the same version as last time (different problem; would need version stamps on cells).
- Per-cell health probe during the sweep (defense-in-depth piece is fizeau-XXXX, the consecutive-failure halt bead).
- Adding the smoke check to non-TerminalBench benchmark scripts.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With sindri-llamacpp configured to use `reasoning: low` (the broken state we just fixed), `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"` (or equivalent error text) and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. With sindri-llamacpp correctly configured (no `reasoning` param), the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. Test by killing the llama-server process: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` (or curl error 7) and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. The cloud lane skip works: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call openrouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.

Cost budget: at most ~30s × N_lanes added to sweep startup wall time. For an 11-lane full sweep that's ~5 min, much cheaper than waiting for the first cell of each broken lane to fail.","events_attachment":"fizeau-586e476a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T06:14:09.133790752Z","id":"fizeau-586e476a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:benchmark","kind:reliability","kind:tooling"],"notes":"Operator made ACs mechanically verifiable with explicit test names and cleared stale AC-quality gate metadata.","owner":"erik","parent":"fizeau-1a5353cd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-62bb603b","status":"closed","title":"bench: preflight smoke test for each lane in run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh","updated_at":"2026-05-12T06:30:46.511566804Z"}

The bead commit contains the ADR document and no implementation files; verify with `git show --name-only --format= HEAD` after the worker commit.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T12:10:11Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1864078","closing_commit_sha":"d4b2bac1875b0b4babb8a349308ecfdd8a0265f8","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:03:50.421530971Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-5b6512ef","depends_on_id":"fizeau-c04be6b0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:03:56Z"},{"issue_id":"fizeau-5b6512ef","depends_on_id":"fizeau-d18e11f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:03:56Z"}],"description":"Design ADR (no implementation in this bead) for cost-based routing
where quota-remaining is a factor in the cost score. Implementation
follows in a separate EPIC after the ADR is accepted.

Per operator direction 2026-05-11:

> For a given model power, we need to intelligently choose based on
> "cost", where remaining usage quota impacts the cost.

Cost model (proposed for ADR-011):

- **Subscription with quota remaining**: effective cost ≈ 0 (or
  quota-fraction-aware: lower-cost the more quota remains).
- **Subscription with quota exhausted**: drop from candidates.
- **Per-token API**: effective cost = catalog `cost_input_per_m` ×
  estimated input tokens + `cost_output_per_m` × estimated output
  tokens.
- **Local provider** (free): effective cost = 0 if eligible (per bead
  D's IncludeByDefault filter), else dropped.

Quota signal sources:

- HTTP rate-limit response headers (per recent commit 7776890e —
  `quota_exhausted` already plumbed).
- Subscription `/usage` endpoints when available.
- In-memory recent-rejection rate as fallback.

Quota-pool semantic (from bead E1):

- Models declare a `quota_pool` (defaulting to provider system).
- Pool exhaustion drops all models in that pool from the candidate set.
- Models in a different pool (e.g., `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` in
  `openai-codex-spark`) become eligible as fallbacks even if not
  version-newest, satisfying the "use spark when main pool is empty"
  operator request.

Power-floor semantic:

- Caller / DDx specifies required `MinPower` (and optional `MaxPower`).
- Routing filters candidates to `MinPower ≤ candidate.power ≤ MaxPower`.
- Among survivors, pick min effective cost.
- Tie-break: prefer subscription over PAYG; prefer lower-power if it
  exceeds requirement (cheapest qualified candidate, not most powerful).

## ADR scope

The ADR document itself, written to
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md`.
Standard ADR structure: Context, Decision, Consequences, Out of scope,
References.

## Decision points the ADR must resolve

1. Quota fraction → cost mapping (linear? step function? thresholds?).
2. Quota window time handling (do we reserve quota for later? probably
   not in v1 — use available now).
3. Token estimate source (the request itself? a heuristic? a learned
   model?). v1 likely uses a coarse system-prompt-length-based
   estimate.
4. Fallback chain ordering when quota exhausts mid-request (single try?
   retry on alternate?).
5. How `IncludeByDefault: false` (bead D) composes with cost ranking
   (drops from default candidate set entirely; not just deprioritized).

## In-scope files

- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md
  (new)

## Out of scope

- Implementation (separate EPIC after ADR acceptance).
- Quota signal infrastructure beyond what 7776890e already provides
  (any new signals needed get filed as their own beads under the EPIC).
- Per-tenant or per-key quota tracking (flag as future scope in the

+{"acceptance":"1. `TestPreflightLaneMisconfiguredReasoning` covers sindri-llamacpp configured with `reasoning: low`: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero in under 60s with a `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED` message naming the unsupported reasoning parameter and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. `TestPreflightLaneOK` covers correctly configured sindri-llamacpp: the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. `TestPreflightLaneConnectionRefused` covers stopped llama-server: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` or curl error 7, and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. `TestPreflightManagedCloudSkipped` covers cloud lanes: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call OpenRouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.
5. `scripts/benchmark/test_preflight_lane.bash` or an equivalent shellspec file defines the four test cases above against a mock HTTP server.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T06:14:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1864078","closing_commit_sha":"aeb009ff91eb5926da7513c1dceccb386328fcc9","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:45:58.814386643Z","description":"Add a per-lane preflight check to scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh that fires a single 1-token chat completion against each requested lane's endpoint before any matrix runner spawns. If the endpoint cannot produce a token within 30s using the lane's actual sampling params, fail the sweep with a non-zero exit and a clear message naming the lane, the endpoint, the model id, and the underlying error.

Why this matters: see parent epic. The 2026-05-10 sindri-llamacpp incident wasted 119 cells (~93 min) on a `reasoning: low` parameter the llama-server provider rejects. The same hour, sindri-vllm wasted multiple cells on a hung model engine where /v1/models responded but /v1/chat/completions never streamed tokens. A single curl-to-completion check per lane catches all these classes (plus auth failures, missing-model 404s, connection-refused, wrong content-type) at sweep startup, before any matrix runner spawns.

Behavior:

- Fire after `Resolved tasks:` is printed, before the per-lane "starting" markers.
- For each lane in the resolved plan: build a JSON body using the lane's `fizeau_env.FIZEAU_MODEL` and the lane's sampling block (temperature, top_p, top_k, reasoning when present). POST to `<base_url>/chat/completions` with `max_tokens: 1` and a 30s timeout.
- Pass condition: the response is JSON-parseable and `.choices[0].message.content` is a non-null string (even an empty string is OK — the model started decoding).
- Fail condition: any non-2xx, timeout, malformed JSON, or missing message content. Print the lane id, base_url, model id, sampling params attempted, and the response body (truncated to 1KB).
- Skip preflight for managed-cloud lanes that have always-on SLAs (openrouter, openai, anthropic) — these are well-known and a preflight just burns quota. Identify by checking the resource group's `provider_type` against an allow-list (vllm, llama-server, omlx, lmstudio, rapid-mlx, ollama, lucebox).

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh — add preflight_lane() function + per-lane invocation loop right before the existing per-lane plan output.
- scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml — no schema change needed; the existing `fizeau_env` and `sampling` blocks are sufficient.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Pre-resume validation that the model server is the same version as last time (different problem; would need version stamps on cells).
- Per-cell health probe during the sweep (defense-in-depth piece is fizeau-XXXX, the consecutive-failure halt bead).
- Adding the smoke check to non-TerminalBench benchmark scripts.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With sindri-llamacpp configured to use `reasoning: low` (the broken state we just fixed), `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp,vidar --dry-run` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: reasoning="low" is not supported by provider type "llama-server"` (or equivalent error text) and does not print any per-lane "starting" marker.
2. With sindri-llamacpp correctly configured (no `reasoning` param), the same command prints `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp OK (1 token in <wall>s)` and continues to the lane plan output normally.
3. Test by killing the llama-server process: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes sindri-llamacpp` exits non-zero with `[preflight] sindri-llamacpp FAILED: connection refused` (or curl error 7) and no matrix runner is spawned.
4. The cloud lane skip works: `scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh --phase full --lanes openrouter-qwen36 --dry-run` does not call openrouter and prints `[preflight] openrouter-qwen36 SKIPPED (managed cloud lane)`.

Cost budget: at most ~30s × N_lanes added to sweep startup wall time. 2. service_refresh_scheduler.go owns a debounced ticker per registered QuotaHarness firing at QuotaFreshness()/2 and per AccountHarness whose AccountFreshness() differs from quota
3. Scheduler discovers harnesses by iterating service.harnessByName over the registry and type-asserting to QuotaHarness / AccountHarness independently
4. Scheduler reads QuotaStatus before each potential refresh to obtain CapturedAt; on cache-cold (State=QuotaUnavailable) the scheduler always calls RefreshQuota
5. Scheduler skips a tick when last-known QuotaStatus.CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now (cache-freshness dedup is the scheduler's job, not RefreshQuota's)
6. Startup refresh kickoff fires once on scheduler construction for every registered QuotaHarness / AccountHarness with a cache-cold state, per the cache-cold path in primary-harness-capability-baseline.md
7. Fake-clock tests prove debounce cadence, single-flight respect via cache lock, startup kickoff, and clean ctx cancel
8. The runner errors when --lanes intersected with selected phase(s) yields empty (cmd/bench/sweep.go:773 "none of --lanes ... are present in selected phase(s)"). Today's incident: `--lanes fiz-sindri-lucebox-qwen3-6-27b --phase all` aborted because `all` iterates phases lucebox isn't enrolled in.

Target: introduce subsets[] (pure task lists) and recipes[] (curated lane+subset bundles, pure CLI sugar). Runner's matrix becomes subset × lanes; no YAML enrollment constraint. CLI gains --subset and --recipe; --phase becomes a deprecated alias. lanes[].phases is removed (always dead data per sweep.go:71-84). resource_groups[].runner_jobs_default added to replace the wrapper's per-phase MATRIX_JOBS_MANAGED mapping.

Plan reviewed across two independent fresh-eyes passes (internal Plan agent + Codex CLI); v3 spec in commit-side branch notes. where quota-remaining is a factor in the cost score. Implementation
follows in a separate EPIC after the ADR is accepted.

Per operator direction 2026-05-11:

> For a given model power, we need to intelligently choose based on
> "cost", where remaining usage quota impacts the cost.

Cost model (proposed for ADR-011):

- **Subscription with quota remaining**: effective cost ≈ 0 (or
  quota-fraction-aware: lower-cost the more quota remains).
- **Subscription with quota exhausted**: drop from candidates.
- **Per-token API**: effective cost = catalog `cost_input_per_m` ×
  estimated input tokens + `cost_output_per_m` × estimated output
  tokens.
- **Local provider** (free): effective cost = 0 if eligible (per bead
  D's IncludeByDefault filter), else dropped.

Quota signal sources:

- HTTP rate-limit response headers (per recent commit 7776890e —
  `quota_exhausted` already plumbed).
- Subscription `/usage` endpoints when available.
- In-memory recent-rejection rate as fallback.

Quota-pool semantic (from bead E1):

- Models declare a `quota_pool` (defaulting to provider system).
- Pool exhaustion drops all models in that pool from the candidate set.
- Models in a different pool (e.g., `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` in
  `openai-codex-spark`) become eligible as fallbacks even if not
  version-newest, satisfying the "use spark when main pool is empty"
  operator request.

Power-floor semantic:

- Caller / DDx specifies required `MinPower` (and optional `MaxPower`).
- Routing filters candidates to `MinPower ≤ candidate.power ≤ MaxPower`.
- Among survivors, pick min effective cost.
- Tie-break: prefer subscription over PAYG; prefer lower-power if it
  exceeds requirement (cheapest qualified candidate, not most powerful).

## ADR scope

The ADR document itself, written to
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md`.
Standard ADR structure: Context, Decision, Consequences, Out of scope,
References.

## Decision points the ADR must resolve

1. Quota fraction → cost mapping (linear? step function? thresholds?).
2. Quota window time handling (do we reserve quota for later? probably
   not in v1 — use available now).
3. Token estimate source (the request itself? a heuristic? a learned
   model?). v1 likely uses a coarse system-prompt-length-based
   estimate.
4. Fallback chain ordering when quota exhausts mid-request (single try?
   retry on alternate?).
5. How `IncludeByDefault: false` (bead D) composes with cost ranking
   (drops from default candidate set entirely; not just deprioritized).

## In-scope files

- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md
  (new)

## Out of scope

- Implementation (separate EPIC after ADR acceptance).
- Quota signal infrastructure beyond what 7776890e already provides
  (any new signals needed get filed as their own beads under the EPIC).
- Per-tenant or per-key quota tracking (flag as future scope in the
  ADR).","events":[{"actor":"","body":"[{"ac":1,"text":"`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-011-cost-based-routing-with-quota-pools.md` exists and has the standard ADR structure (Context, Decision,  {"acceptance":"1. Child beads add plan/profile validation, a lane authoring command or generator, and DS4 MTP-specific wiring.

3. Tracker notes identify the final operator command for adding a lane and the verification command to run before starting a sweep.","created_at":"2026-05-15T00:42:49.220313689Z","description":"Problem
Adding a TerminalBench lane currently requires hand-editing several independent surfaces: scripts/benchmark/terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml lane entries, recipe lane lists, scripts/benchmark/profiles/<id>.yaml, and shell aliases in scripts/benchmark/run_terminalbench_2_1_sweep.sh. The fiz-vidar-ds4-mtp incident shows this is too easy to leave half-wired: the lane existed, but its profile was missing, recipes did not include it, and its short alias did not exist.

Goal
Make lane addition a single, validated workflow. An operator should be able to add or clone a lane, validate it before running a sweep, and get precise diagnostics when the profile, recipe enrollment, resource group, alias, or runtime switch is missing.

Out of scope

Committed to master.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T00:44:59Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"525134","closing_commit_sha":"db57a86150127c088438d3ac75bfae9d0890eebd","created_at":"2026-05-12T00:44:35.644948769Z","description":"Design ADR for the per-source on-disk cache that backs the available-
models snapshot. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, force-refresh behavior, TTLs,
crash recovery, and the mandatory test matrix for the implementation.

This is design-only. Implementation is bead M1.

## ADR scope (sections to write)

### Context
- The `fiz models` snapshot needs live data from multiple sources
  (provider /v1/models, harness PTY, /props introspection, /usage
  endpoints, rate-limit response headers).
- Discovery sources are slow (PTY ~30s, OR /api/v1/models ~316ms).
- Runtime signals (quota, status, latency) change frequently.
- UI must never hang (100ms budget).
- Multi-process: `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `fiz models`, routing all run
  concurrently and must share cache state.
- fizeau already has a battle-tested file-locking idiom in
  `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` (PID + atomic-create-or-fail
  with crash recovery via PID-alive check). Build on it.

### Decision

#### File layout
```
~/.cache/fizeau/   (os.UserCacheDir())
├── discovery/                          # slow, stable
│   ├── openrouter.json                 # 24h TTL
│   ├── claude-subscription.json        # 24h TTL
│   ├── codex-subscription.json         # 24h TTL
│   ├── vidar-ds4.json                  # 1h TTL (local)
│   └── sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.json  # 1h TTL (local)
└── runtime/                            # hot, volatile
    ├── openrouter.json                 # 5min TTL
    ├── claude-subscription.json        # 5min TTL
    └── ...
```
Each data file is JSON. Keys map to fizeau's canonical model identity:
`<provider>/<model_id>`.

#### Lock + marker pattern (two-tier)
- `<source>.lock`: brief mutation lock. Same idiom as
  `acquireMatrixLock` — `O_CREATE|O_EXCL`, JSON `{PID, StartedAt}`,
  crash recovery via `processAlive(PID)`. Held during state
  transitions only (claim refresh, finish refresh).
- `<source>.refreshing`: long-lived refresh marker. JSON
  `{PID, StartedAt, Deadline}`. Held during refresh IO. Other
  processes see this and wait OR return stale.

#### Algorithms (full pseudocode in ADR)
1. `claim_refresh(source) -> ClaimedByMe | AlreadyInFlight(marker)`
2. `refresh_and_commit(source)` — IO, atomic-rename, marker cleanup
3. `wait_for_refresh(source, marker, max_wait)` — poll marker
4. `read(source) -> (data, fresh_bool)` — UI never blocks
5. `force_refresh(source)` — sync; wait or claim
6. In-process single-flight via `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight`
   (composes with file-based marker for cross-process)

#### TTL defaults (operator-configurable via env or config)
- Discovery PTY: 24h (PTY is slow; refresh rare)
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models: 24h
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models (local): 1h
- Runtime signals: 5 min
- Lock acquisition timeout: 100ms
- Refresh deadline: 60s for PTY, 10s for HTTP discovery, 5s for runtime
- Marker staleness threshold: 2× refresh deadline

#### Crash recovery
- Stale `.refreshing` markers (past deadline OR dead PID) are
  presumed orphan; next caller overrides. No manual cleanup needed.
- Stale `.lock` files (dead PID) are removed on next attempt
  (existing `acquireMatrixLock` semantics).

#### Force-refresh semantics
- Synchronous. Waits for in-flight refresh to complete (up to
  refresh deadline). Then reads fresh data.
- Bypasses TTL check.

#### Cache prune
- `fiz cache prune` removes data files for sources not in current
  config. Called explicitly OR auto-pruned at startup (operator
  decision; ADR proposes manual to avoid surprises).

### Mandatory test matrix (10 tests)

The implementation bead (M1) MUST ship all 10 tests passing. Skipping
any = the cache is dangerous. Spawn child Go processes for multi-
process scenarios (standard `TestMain` + `os.Args[0]` -test.run
helper-process pattern).

1. **Concurrent claim from 2 procs**: exactly one ClaimedByMe.
2. **Reader during refresh**: atomic rename → no torn read; reader
   sees old until rename, new after.
3. **Crash during refresh** (kill -9): next process detects dead PID
   in marker, claims.
4. **Refresh timeout**: marker deadline expires, next process
   claims; orig process's late commit rejected (lock check).
5. **Force-refresh waits + reads fresh**.
6. **Concurrent normal+force**: normal returns stale immediately,
   background refresh single-flighted with force.
7. **Atomic rename verified**: 100 concurrent readers + 1 writer
   producing 100 versions; every read is a complete consistent
   version (checksum).
8. **`fiz cache prune` doesn't race active sources**.
9. **Stale-while-revalidate**: stale read returns immediately,
   background refresh fires.
10. **PID reuse safety**: marker PID gets reused by unrelated
    process; deadline check is the safety net.

### Consequences

**Positive:**
- UI never hangs (read path is always cache-bounded < 100ms).
- Multi-process safe across `ddx work` and `fiz` invocations.
- Crash-safe via PID-alive + deadline checks.
- Reuses fizeau's proven `acquireMatrixLock` idiom; no new
  cross-platform lock library required.
- Each source has independent lifecycle; OR re-discovery doesn't
  block PTY discovery.

**Negative:**
- Two file roles per source (lock + marker) is more complexity
  than a single mutex.
- File-based coordination has inherent edge cases (PID reuse,
  filesystem semantics differences); test matrix is non-trivial.
- Stale data may be served briefly during refresh (acceptable
  trade for non-blocking UI).

### Out of scope
- Persistent storage of historical signals (cache is per-source,
  current-state only).
- Multi-machine cache sharing (per-host only).
- Compression (JSON, plain).
- Encryption at rest.
- OR sub-provider cache (deferred M5).

### References
- Bead A (`fizeau-b2c5c826`) — version-aware ranker, parses model IDs
- Bead D (`fizeau-d18e11f5`) — IncludeByDefault filter (composes with
  AutoRoutable in snapshot)
- Bead E1 (`fizeau-c04be6b0`) — quota_pool catalog field (consumed in
  M2 enrichment)
- Bead E2 (`fizeau-5b6512ef`) — ADR-011 cost-based routing (downstream
  consumer of snapshot)
- `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` — prior-art lock idiom
- `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_cache.go` — atomic-rename
  prior art

## In-scope files
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-012-per-source-on-disk-cache.md (new)

## Out of scope

Misconfigured fixture sweep exits code 75 after exactly 5 cells with aborted.json written. 3. --no-consecutive-failure-halt runs all cells without aborting. 4. lane_aborted appears as a known invalid class in matrix.json's invalid_by_class breakdown.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T07:37:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1864078","closing_commit_sha":"226199fbb15473abf05e970d8b079a36cf678c46","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:46:32.72270603Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-72d18004","depends_on_id":"fizeau-586e476a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:46:45Z"}],"description":"Add a consecutive-failure halt to the matrix runner (cmd/bench/matrix.go) that aborts the entire lane after N=5 cells fail with byte-identical error fingerprints in a row. Writes a single `lane_aborted` summary cell explaining why; subsequent cells in the lane are skipped (not run, not written).

Why this matters: see parent epic. The preflight smoke test (sibling bead fizeau-586e476a) catches misconfigured lanes at sweep startup. This bead is the defense-in-depth piece for cases where preflight passed but the endpoint dies mid-sweep — e.g. vLLM model engine enters a degraded state after running fine for a while, or a model server gets OOM-killed and the wrapper restarts it broken. Today such regressions cause every remaining cell in the lane to fail identically until human intervention.

Behavior:

- After each cell in a lane completes, compute a fingerprint = SHA256 of the report's `error` field (truncated to first 256 bytes) + `final_status`. Store the last N=5 fingerprints in lane-scoped state inside the existing per-lane matrix runner process (no on-disk state).
- When 5 consecutive cells share the same fingerprint AND are all `graded_fail` or `harness_crash` (not `graded_pass`), halt the lane:
  * Write one synthetic `report.json` to a path like `<cells_root>/.lane_aborted/<lane_id>/aborted.json` with `final_status: "lane_aborted"`, `error: "halted after 5 consecutive identical failures: <fingerprint preview>"`, plus the fingerprint and the list of the 5 task ids that triggered.
  * Print `[matrix] lane=<lane_id> ABORTED after 5 consecutive identical failures (last task: <task>): <error preview>` to stderr.
  * Exit the matrix runner with a distinct exit code (e.g. 75 / EX_TEMPFAIL) so the sweep script can attribute the abort to this cause vs a normal lane completion.
- Add `final_status: "lane_aborted"` to the matrix invalid-class machinery so the website / aggregates display these explicitly (not as silent missing cells).
- Add a `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag to disable the safety net for operators debugging a known-broken lane.

Failure-mode test cases (must all be covered):

- 5 cells with identical `agent: provider error: ...` errors → halt fires.
- 5 cells with identical `harness_crash: context canceled` (the docker subnet exhaustion pattern from earlier in this codebase) → halt fires.
- 4 identical errors then 1 graded_pass → halt does NOT fire; counter resets.
- 4 identical errors then a 5th error with even one byte different → halt does NOT fire.
- `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag → halt never fires regardless.

In-scope files:
- cmd/bench/matrix.go — add ConsecutiveFailureTracker (or inline state) inside the matrix-runner main loop; integrate into the per-cell write path; add the flag.
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — register `lane_aborted` as a known invalid class (so summarizeMatrixCells counts it correctly, and the regen pipeline treats it as systemic, not model failure).
- cmd/bench/matrix_test.go — add the four behavior tests above using the existing test fixture pattern.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Auto-restart of the lane after the user fixes the issue. (For now, operator manually re-runs.)
- Cross-lane abort cascade (one lane's repeated failures shouldn't halt other lanes).
- Surfacing the halt count in the website data — that's a follow-up once we see how often this fires in practice.

Acceptance is a command:

1. `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestConsecutiveFailureHalt` passes (covers the four behaviors above).
2. With a deliberately-misconfigured fixture profile that produces identical errors, `fiz-bench matrix --profiles broken-fixture --reps 10 --out /tmp/abort-test` exits with code 75 after exactly 5 cells, writes `aborted.json` under `<out>/.lane_aborted/`, and the remaining 5 cells are not present in the output dir.
3. models snapshot. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, force-refresh behavior, TTLs,
crash recovery, and the mandatory test matrix for the implementation.

This is design-only. Implementation is bead M1.

## ADR scope (sections to write)

### Context
- The `fiz models` snapshot needs live data from multiple sources
  (provider /v1/models, harness PTY, /props introspection, /usage
  endpoints, rate-limit response headers).
- Discovery sources are slow (PTY ~30s, OR /api/v1/models ~316ms).
- Runtime signals (quota, status, latency) change frequently.
- UI must never hang (100ms budget).
- Multi-process: `ddx work`, `ddx try`, `fiz models`, routing all run
  concurrently and must share cache state.
- fizeau already has a battle-tested file-locking idiom in
  `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` (PID + atomic-create-or-fail
  with crash recovery via PID-alive check). Build on it.

### Decision

#### File layout
```
~/.cache/fizeau/   (os.UserCacheDir())
├── discovery/                          # slow, stable
│   ├── openrouter.json                 # 24h TTL
│   ├── claude-subscription.json        # 24h TTL
│   ├── codex-subscription.json         # 24h TTL
│   ├── vidar-ds4.json                  # 1h TTL (local)
│   └── sindri-club-3090-llamacpp.json  # 1h TTL (local)
└── runtime/                            # hot, volatile
    ├── openrouter.json                 # 5min TTL
    ├── claude-subscription.json        # 5min TTL
    └── ...
```
Each data file is JSON. Keys map to fizeau's canonical model identity:
`<provider>/<model_id>`.

#### Lock + marker pattern (two-tier)
- `<source>.lock`: brief mutation lock. Same idiom as
  `acquireMatrixLock` — `O_CREATE|O_EXCL`, JSON `{PID, StartedAt}`,
  crash recovery via `processAlive(PID)`. Held during state
  transitions only (claim refresh, finish refresh).
- `<source>.refreshing`: long-lived refresh marker. JSON
  `{PID, StartedAt, Deadline}`. Held during refresh IO. Other
  processes see this and wait OR return stale.

#### Algorithms (full pseudocode in ADR)
1. `claim_refresh(source) -> ClaimedByMe | AlreadyInFlight(marker)`
2. `refresh_and_commit(source)` — IO, atomic-rename, marker cleanup
3. `wait_for_refresh(source, marker, max_wait)` — poll marker
4. `read(source) -> (data, fresh_bool)` — UI never blocks
5. `force_refresh(source)` — sync; wait or claim
6. In-process single-flight via `golang.org/x/sync/singleflight`
   (composes with file-based marker for cross-process)

#### TTL defaults (operator-configurable via env or config)
- Discovery PTY: 24h (PTY is slow; refresh rare)
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models: 24h
- Discovery HTTP /v1/models (local): 1h
- Runtime signals: 5 min
- Lock acquisition timeout: 100ms
- Refresh deadline: 60s for PTY, 10s for HTTP discovery, 5s for runtime
- Marker staleness threshold: 2× refresh deadline

#### Crash recovery
- Stale `.refreshing` markers (past deadline OR dead PID) are
  presumed orphan; next caller overrides. No manual cleanup needed.
- Stale `.lock` files (dead PID) are removed on next attempt
  (existing `acquireMatrixLock` semantics).

#### Force-refresh semantics
- Synchronous. Waits for in-flight refresh to complete (up to
  refresh deadline). Then reads fresh data.
- Bypasses TTL check.

#### Cache prune
- `fiz cache prune` removes data files for sources not in current
  config. Called explicitly OR auto-pruned at startup (operator
  decision; ADR proposes manual to avoid surprises).

### Mandatory test matrix (10 tests)

The implementation bead (M1) MUST ship all 10 tests passing. Skipping
any = the cache is dangerous. Spawn child Go processes for multi-
process scenarios (standard `TestMain` + `os.Args[0]` -test.run
helper-process pattern).

1. **Concurrent claim from 2 procs**: exactly one ClaimedByMe.
2. **Reader during refresh**: atomic rename → no torn read; reader
   sees old until rename, new after.
3. **Crash during refresh** (kill -9): next process detects dead PID
   in marker, claims.
4. **Refresh timeout**: marker deadline expires, next process
   claims; orig process's late commit rejected (lock check).
5. **Force-refresh waits + reads fresh**.
6. **Concurrent normal+force**: normal returns stale immediately,
   background refresh single-flighted with force.
7. **Atomic rename verified**: 100 concurrent readers + 1 writer
   producing 100 versions; every read is a complete consistent
   version (checksum).
8. **`fiz cache prune` doesn't race active sources**.
9. **Stale-while-revalidate**: stale read returns immediately,
   background refresh fires.
10. **PID reuse safety**: marker PID gets reused by unrelated
    process; deadline check is the safety net.

### Consequences

**Positive:**
- UI never hangs (read path is always cache-bounded < 100ms).
- Multi-process safe across `ddx work` and `fiz` invocations.
- Crash-safe via PID-alive + deadline checks.
- Reuses fizeau's proven `acquireMatrixLock` idiom; no new
  cross-platform lock library required.
- Each source has independent lifecycle; OR re-discovery doesn't
  block PTY discovery.

**Negative:**
- Two file roles per source (lock + marker) is more complexity
  than a single mutex.
- File-based coordination has inherent edge cases (PID reuse,
  filesystem semantics differences); test matrix is non-trivial.
- Stale data may be served briefly during refresh (acceptable
  trade for non-blocking UI).

### Out of scope
- Persistent storage of historical signals (cache is per-source,
  current-state only).
- Multi-machine cache sharing (per-host only).
- Compression (JSON, plain).
- Encryption at rest.
- OR sub-provider cache (deferred M5).

### References
- Bead A (`fizeau-b2c5c826`) — version-aware ranker, parses model IDs
- Bead D (`fizeau-d18e11f5`) — IncludeByDefault filter (composes with
  AutoRoutable in snapshot)
- Bead E1 (`fizeau-c04be6b0`) — quota_pool catalog field (consumed in
  M2 enrichment)
- Bead E2 (`fizeau-5b6512ef`) — ADR-011 cost-based routing (downstream
  consumer of snapshot)
- `cmd/bench/matrix.go:acquireMatrixLock` — prior-art lock idiom
- `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_cache.go` — atomic-rename
  prior art

Misconfigured fixture sweep exits code 75 after exactly 5 cells with aborted.json written. 3. --no-consecutive-failure-halt runs all cells without aborting. 4. lane_aborted appears as a known invalid class in matrix.json's invalid_by_class breakdown.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T07:37:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1864078","closing_commit_sha":"226199fbb15473abf05e970d8b079a36cf678c46","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:46:32.72270603Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-72d18004","depends_on_id":"fizeau-586e476a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T02:46:45Z"}],"description":"Add a consecutive-failure halt to the matrix runner (cmd/bench/matrix.go) that aborts the entire lane after N=5 cells fail with byte-identical error fingerprints in a row. Writes a single `lane_aborted` summary cell explaining why; subsequent cells in the lane are skipped (not run, not written).

Why this matters: see parent epic. The preflight smoke test (sibling bead fizeau-586e476a) catches misconfigured lanes at sweep startup. This bead is the defense-in-depth piece for cases where preflight passed but the endpoint dies mid-sweep — e.g. vLLM model engine enters a degraded state after running fine for a while, or a model server gets OOM-killed and the wrapper restarts it broken. Today such regressions cause every remaining cell in the lane to fail identically until human intervention.

Behavior:

- After each cell in a lane completes, compute a fingerprint = SHA256 of the report's `error` field (truncated to first 256 bytes) + `final_status`. Store the last N=5 fingerprints in lane-scoped state inside the existing per-lane matrix runner process (no on-disk state).
- When 5 consecutive cells share the same fingerprint AND are all `graded_fail` or `harness_crash` (not `graded_pass`), halt the lane:
  * Write one synthetic `report.json` to a path like `<cells_root>/.lane_aborted/<lane_id>/aborted.json` with `final_status: "lane_aborted"`, `error: "halted after 5 consecutive identical failures: <fingerprint preview>"`, plus the fingerprint and the list of the 5 task ids that triggered.
  * Print `[matrix] lane=<lane_id> ABORTED after 5 consecutive identical failures (last task: <task>): <error preview>` to stderr.
  * Exit the matrix runner with a distinct exit code (e.g. 75 / EX_TEMPFAIL) so the sweep script can attribute the abort to this cause vs a normal lane completion.
- Add `final_status: "lane_aborted"` to the matrix invalid-class machinery so the website / aggregates display these explicitly (not as silent missing cells).
- Add a `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag to disable the safety net for operators debugging a known-broken lane.

Failure-mode test cases (must all be covered):

- 5 cells with identical `agent: provider error: ...` errors → halt fires.
- 5 cells with identical `harness_crash: context canceled` (the docker subnet exhaustion pattern from earlier in this codebase) → halt fires.
- 4 identical errors then 1 graded_pass → halt does NOT fire; counter resets.
- 4 identical errors then a 5th error with even one byte different → halt does NOT fire.
- `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` flag → halt never fires regardless.

In-scope files:
- cmd/bench/matrix.go — add ConsecutiveFailureTracker (or inline state) inside the matrix-runner main loop; integrate into the per-cell write path; add the flag.
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — register `lane_aborted` as a known invalid class (so summarizeMatrixCells counts it correctly, and the regen pipeline treats it as systemic, not model failure).
- cmd/bench/matrix_test.go — add the four behavior tests above using the existing test fixture pattern.

Out-of-scope (file separate beads):
- Auto-restart of the lane after the user fixes the issue. (For now, operator manually re-runs.)
- Cross-lane abort cascade (one lane's repeated failures shouldn't halt other lanes).
- Surfacing the halt count in the website data — that's a follow-up once we see how often this fires in practice.

Acceptance is a command:

1. `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestConsecutiveFailureHalt` passes (covers the four behaviors above).
2. With a deliberately-misconfigured fixture profile that produces identical errors, `fiz-bench matrix --profiles broken-fixture --reps 10 --out /tmp/abort-test` exits with code 75 after exactly 5 cells, writes `aborted.json` under `<out>/.lane_aborted/`, and the remaining 5 cells are not present in the output dir.
3. The same fixture with `--no-consecutive-failure-halt` runs all 10 cells to completion (each failing) without providers (incl harnesses-as-providers), reading per-source caches
(via M1), parsing model IDs via the version-aware ranker (bead A), and
joining with catalog metadata (power, cost, reasoning_levels,
quota_pool from bead E1).

## Public API sketch

```go
package modelregistry

type Source string
const (
    SourceNativeAPI    Source = "native_api"
    SourceHarnessPTY   Source = "harness_pty"
    SourcePropsAPI     Source = "props_api"
)

type ModelStatus string
const (
    StatusAvailable     ModelStatus = "available"
    StatusRateLimited   ModelStatus = "rate_limited"
    StatusUnreachable   ModelStatus = "unreachable"
    StatusUnknown       ModelStatus = "unknown"
)

type KnownModel struct {
    // Identity (per ADR-012; matches ModelRef)
    Provider      string
    ID            string

    // How fizeau learned of it
    DiscoveredVia Source
    DiscoveredAt  time.Time

    // Parsed via bead A
    Family        string
    Version       []int
    Tier          modelcatalog.Tier
    PreRelease    bool

    // From catalog join
    Power            int
    CostInputPerM    float64
    CostOutputPerM   float64
    ContextWindow    int
    ReasoningLevels  []string
    QuotaPool        string  // E1

    // Composed eligibility (consumed by routing)
    AutoRoutable    bool      // catalog × IncludeByDefault × Status
    ExclusionReason string
}

type ModelSnapshot struct {
    Models  []KnownModel
    AsOf    time.Time
    Sources map[string]SourceMeta
}

type SourceMeta struct {
    LastRefreshedAt time.Time
    Stale           bool
    Error           string
}

func Assemble(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, cat *modelcatalog.Catalog, cache *discoverycache.Cache) (ModelSnapshot, error)
```

## Provider enumeration

Iterates `cfg.Providers` (existing user config) plus implicit
subscription harnesses (claude-subscription, codex-subscription) when
their CLIs are installed and authenticated.

## Per-source discovery

Each provider type has a discovery adapter:
- openai-compat (openrouter, openai, vidar-ds4, sindri-llamacpp): GET
  `/v1/models` via cache
- ds4: also GET `/props`
- claude / codex harnesses: PTY-based cassette read via cache (existing
  model_discovery.go code, now wrapped through M1's cache)

Adapters return `[]string` (model IDs); assembler produces
`KnownModel` per ID.

## Tier ranker integration

Each model ID parsed via `modelcatalog.tiers.Parse(id)` (bead A). Sets
Family/Version/Tier/PreRelease.

## Catalog join

For each (provider, model) candidate, look up the catalog entry. If
present, populate Power/Cost/ContextWindow/ReasoningLevels/QuotaPool.
If not, leave zero/empty (snapshot still includes the entry —
provider-discovered models without catalog metadata are still
routable, just with limited info).

## AutoRoutable composition

`AutoRoutable = catalog.AutoRoutable() AND providerConfig.IncludeByDefault
AND status != StatusUnreachable AND status != StatusUnknown`. (Per
bead D's `IncludeByDefault` filter wiring.)

## Catalog-only suppression

Per operator direction: if a catalog model is NOT discovered by any
configured provider, it does NOT appear in the snapshot. The catalog
is implementation detail.

## In-scope files

- internal/modelregistry/types.go — KnownModel, ModelSnapshot, etc.
- internal/modelregistry/assemble.go — top-level Assemble function
- internal/modelregistry/discovery.go — per-provider-type adapters
  (uses existing code in internal/provider/openai/discovery.go and
  internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/model_discovery.go through M1's
  cache wrapper)
- internal/modelregistry/enrich.go — catalog join + AutoRoutable
- internal/modelregistry/assemble_test.go
- internal/modelregistry/discovery_test.go
- internal/modelregistry/enrich_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache + ParseModelRef) implemented
- Bead A (`fizeau-b2c5c826`, version-aware ranker)
- Bead E1 (`fizeau-c04be6b0`, quota_pool field)

## Out of scope

- Runtime signal collection (M3 — Status comes from M3's runtime cache).

Fixing startup-skipped providers to remain unknown is necessary, but route correctness must not depend on a background worker eventually probing them. FEAT-004 and CONTRACT-003 define routing as `route(client_inputs, fiz_models_snapshot)` with `ResolveRoute`/`Execute` refreshing routing-relevant stale fields before scoring. If a local provider has no fresh health/discovery evidence because startup skipped it, automatic routing must not blindly dispatch to it as if it were healthy. It must either obtain fresh-enough evidence through the coordinated refresh/probe path or record a refresh failure/timeout in route evidence before using stale data.

Spec context
- FEAT-004 AC-08: ResolveRoute and Execute refresh stale routing-relevant snapshot fields through the lock-coordinated refresh path before scoring.
- CONTRACT-003: Fizeau has no required daemon; long-running DDx heartbeat is an optimization, not a correctness dependency.
- The May 12 auto-routing plan amendment says autorouting refreshes stale routing-relevant snapshot fields synchronously before scoring while `fiz models` remains quick by default.

In-scope files
- service_routing.go / route-time input assembly
- service_aliveness.go only for small helpers shared with route-time freshness
- service_routing_test.go and/or service_aliveness_test.go
- internal/modelsnapshot tests only if needed for evidence propagation

Out of scope
- Reintroducing request-time probe-on-every-run for already-fresh providers
- Changing score weights
- Changing `fiz models` default stale-while-revalidate behavior

Implementation guidance
Add a route-time freshness check for local provider health/discovery evidence that covers the `unknown because never probed/skipped` state. The check should be bounded and use the existing coordinated refresh/probe mechanics. Successful evidence should allow the candidate; failed/timed-out evidence should become typed candidate evidence (`endpoint_unreachable`, `unhealthy`, or refresh_failed as appropriate). Explicit provider pins remain the operator escape hatch subject to existing policy requirements.","id":"fizeau-a54e0299","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:routing","area:service","area:routehealth","kind:bug","spec:FEAT-004"],"notes":"Dependent route-time freshness bead remains open and blocked on closing fizeau-8e1661b5 and fizeau-828b0a7c.  {"acceptance":"1. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel` proving `resolveExecuteRoute(ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"codex", Policy:"default", Prompt:"x"})` returns a Codex-supported model, not `qwen3.5-27b` or any non-Codex model.
2. Add `TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape` proving `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` are copied from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` path and affect routing candidates.
3. Add `TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch` proving a subprocess harness decision with an unsupported model returns a routing/validation error before the runner is invoked.
4. `fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` succeeds or fails with a pre-dispatch no-viable-route error; it must not invoke Codex with `qwen3.5-27b`.
5. `go test ./internal/routing/... -run "HarnessPolicy|PromptShape|UnsupportedEngineDecision" -count=1` passes.
6. `go test . -run "TestExecuteHarnessPolicyDefaultSelectsCodexSupportedModel|TestResolveExecuteRouteWithEngineForwardsPromptShape|TestExecuteHarnessPolicyRejectsUnsupportedEngineDecisionBeforeDispatch" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T05:21:54.541792134Z","description":"PROBLEM
`fiz run --harness codex --policy default -p "Reply with OK." --max-iter 1 --json` routes to an unsupported model and fails before useful DDx work can start: `model "qwen3.5-27b" is not supported by harness "codex"; supported models: gpt, gpt-5, gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini`. This blocks DDx `ddx work --watch --harness codex --profile default` from using the standard policy band without an explicit model pin.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`service_execute.go:198-211` intentionally sends empty-model subprocess harness requests with Policy/MinPower through the routing engine, but the engine path is not enforcing the selected model against the subprocess harness before dispatch. `service_routing.go:1732-1745` also drops `ServiceExecuteRequest.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` when constructing the `RouteRequest`, so Execute and ResolveRoute can disagree on prompt/tool capability gates. Finally, `internal/routing/gating.go:128-135` treats any explicit Harness as a full bypass of catalog power eligibility, even when Policy/MinPower are the only model-selection input and the engine is supposed to choose within that harness's eligible models.

PROPOSED FIX
Make harness+policy empty-model Execute routing produce only harness-supported concrete models before subprocess dispatch. Preserve explicit model pins and provider-routed harness semantics, but when `Harness` is set and `Model` is empty, apply policy/min-power selection within that harness's supported model set. Forward `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` from `ServiceExecuteRequest` into the `RouteRequest` used by `resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine`. Add pre-dispatch validation so any engine decision for a subprocess harness fails with a typed routing error before invoking the runner if the resolved model is outside that harness allow-list.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change DDx routing policy. Do not add legacy `standard` policy aliases. Do not remove the existing explicit `--model gpt-5.4` workaround from operators; this bead is only the Fizeau routing fix.","id":"fizeau-aedf001b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:routing","area:harness","kind:bug","downstream:ddx"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"routing: constrain harness policy auto-resolution to supported models","updated_at":"2026-05-15T05:21:54.541792134Z"}
-{"acceptance":"1. TestStageDirForcesGitIgnored: create a temp git repo with .gitignore covering a subdir, call StageDir on it, verify files are staged.
   cd cli && go test -run TestStageDirForcesGitIgnored ./internal/agent/...

2. TestEvidenceCommitSucceedsWhenGitignored: Land() end-to-end in a fixture repo whose .gitignore covers .ddx/executions/; verify status=="landed".
   cd cli && go test -run TestEvidenceCommitSucceedsWhenGitignored ./internal/agent/...

3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green

4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","created_at":"2026-05-10T03:21:27.782506519Z","description":"## Problem

Projects that include .ddx/executions/ in their .gitignore (including fizeau and
any repo using the standard DDx .gitignore template) silently fail to land any bead.
The failure is invisible: the implementation is correct, tests pass, but agent.Land()
preserves instead of landing on every attempt.

## Root Cause

cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701 — RealLandingGitOps.StageDir:

  args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
  out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()

git add without --force silently skips gitignored paths (exit 0, no output, no error).
When .ddx/executions/ is gitignored, StageDir stages nothing. CommitStaged sees an
empty index and returns sha="". landEvidence calls evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles which
also returns false (gitignored files not tracked), so it returns error:
"no staged evidence files under .ddx/executions/...". preserveAfterEvidenceFailure
fires, rolls back master, and creates a preserve ref.

Compounding issue: ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps ALL non-pre-merge-check
preserve reasons (including "evidence commit failed") to reasonForStatus="merge
conflict", which ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus returns as "land_conflict" (escalatable).
This causes runEscalatingSingleTierAttempts to burn through the entire model escalation
ladder (min_power 5→7→8→9) before exhausting.

Confirmed in fizeau-bd5193ca: all 4 attempts preserved by evidence commit failure.
Investigation: .ddx/executions/20260510T023250-da87c8c9/fizeau-bd5193ca-investigation.md

## Proposed Fix

Part 1 (fix): In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701, StageDir, change:
  args := append([]string{"add", "--", relPath}, ...)
to:
  args := append([]string{"add", "--force", "--", relPath}, ...)

Evidence files are DDx infrastructure, not user source. They must be committed even
when the project's .gitignore covers .ddx/executions/.

Part 2 (defensive): In execute_bead_status.go, add "evidence commit failed:" as a
non-escalatable prefix in isPreservedNeedsReviewReason so infrastructure failures do
not burn the escalation ladder. Fixing Part 1 eliminates the failure path; Part 2
prevents wasted escalation if any other evidence-commit edge case arises.","events_attachment":"fizeau-af9d5f1c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"bead misfiled — target source files do not exist in this repository ## Summary This bead (fizeau-af9d5f1c) is filed in the fizeau bead queue, but the changes it prescribes belong to the DDx CLI repository (github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx), not to fizeau (github.com/easel/fizeau). An operator must move/refile the bead into the DDx repo's queue, or explicitly redirect it to patch the vendored skill snapshot under `.agents/skills/cli/` here in fizeau. ## Evidence 1. This worktree's `go.mod` declares `module github.com/easel/fizeau`. There is no top-level `cli/` directory: $ ls cli ls: cannot access 'cli': No such file or directory 2. The bead names `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:701` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go`. Neither path exists at the repository root. The only copies present in this repo are read-only skill-bundle mirrors: ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go ./.claude/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go These are vendored snapshots of the DDx CLI source distributed with the DDx skills package; their `go.mod` says `module github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`. They were last touched by commit `d49a2428 chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead 20260508T205324-8992c1f5`, which is a generic checkpoint rather than feature work — consistent with them being a refresh-from-upstream artifact, not a maintenance target. 3. The acceptance criteria assume the DDx repo layout: AC1/AC2: `cd cli && go test -run … ./internal/agent/...` AC3:     `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` `cd cli` is not satisfiable here. The closest analogue (`cd .agents/skills/cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`) builds, but it tests vendored upstream code that fizeau does not run — fizeau invokes the `ddx` binary from PATH. Patching the vendored snapshot would not alter the behavior of the installed `ddx` binary that triggers the bug. 4. Patching the vendored snapshot would also be undone the next time the fizeau repo refreshes its skills bundle from upstream DDx, so any local change there is non-durable by design. 5. The bug itself is real and well-diagnosed (fizeau's `.gitignore` does contain `.ddx/executions/`, confirmed at line 28 of `.gitignore`). It is simply that fizeau is the *consumer* exposed by the bug, while the *fix site* is upstream DDx. ## What an operator needs to do Either: - **(preferred)** Recreate this bead in the DDx CLI repository's bead queue with the same title/description/AC, then close fizeau-af9d5f1c with a pointer to the relocated bead; or - **(only if intentional)** Amend the bead description and AC to specify patching `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` and `.agents/skills/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` here, replace `cd cli` with `cd .agents/skills/cli` in the AC, and acknowledge in the bead that the patch will be overwritten on the next skills refresh from upstream and therefore needs to be mirrored upstream regardless. Without one of these operator decisions, an agent has no scope-legal way to satisfy this bead in the fizeau worktree. ## Why `proposed` and not `open` The blocker is not retryable by re-running an agent: the source files the bead prescribes are not in this repository, and that condition will not change between attempts. A higher-power model would reach the same dead end. Only an operator can refile or rescope the bead.","execute-loop-last-status":"no_changes_operator_required","execute-loop-no-changes-count":2,"execute-loop-suggested-action":"review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work","id":"fizeau-af9d5f1c","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","phase:6"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"fix: StageDir must use git add --force so evidence commits succeed in projects that gitignore .ddx/executions/","updated_at":"2026-05-12T14:00:50.253593425Z"}
