-{"acceptance":"1. TestServiceStartup_ProbesConfiguredProviders asserts that Service.New() runs one TCP-connect probe per configured non-cloud provider within startup-bounded time (e.g. 5s total cap regardless of provider count).
2. TestProbeLoop_RetriesDeadProvidersOnInterval asserts that a provider marked unreachable by probe is re-probed every HealthProbeInterval and recorded back to reachable when it comes online.
3. TestRouter_FiltersUnreachableEndpointsWhenAlternatesExist asserts that when bragi:1234 is recorded unreachable AND claude/sonnet is a valid candidate, the router emits exactly one routing decision selecting claude/sonnet without first dialing bragi.
4. TestRouter_AcceptsUnreachableEndpointWhenSoleCandidate asserts that when the only configured candidate is the unreachable one (explicit operator pin), the router still attempts the request and surfaces the dial failure honestly.
5. TestRouteHealth_PersistsProbeResults — paired with fizeau-7cd66e6b's persistence story, asserts a fresh process reads the prior probe results within HealthSignalTTL.
6. RouteDecision evidence includes per-candidate {last_probe_at, last_probe_success} fields where applicable.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the aliveness probing + new ServiceOptions knobs (HealthProbeInterval, HealthSignalTTL).
8. go test ./internal/routehealth/... ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T00:19:34Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2417331","closing_commit_sha":"443c939d3d38e79260e714d8f0b8c0d75d5f8d9b","created_at":"2026-05-15T00:14:16.355963324Z","description":"PROBLEM
Fizeau has no proactive aliveness check for configured providers. A dead local provider (e.g. bragi:1234, grendel:1234) is only learned to be dead by attempting a request against it and eating the dial timeout. The failure feedback is stored in internal/routehealth.Store, which is:
1. Process-local (sync.RWMutex map; not persisted to disk).
2. Short-TTL (DefaultCooldown = 30s; entries expire and are re-tried).

Result for an operator with bragi unplugged but configured:
- Every fresh `ddx work` or `ddx try` process re-probes bragi on its first routing decision, eating a 5s dial timeout (post fizeau-b5757624) or 30s pre-fix.
- Even within a long-running `ddx work --watch`, the route exclusion expires every 30s and bragi gets re-attempted on the next cycle.
- Aggregate cost over a 27-bead drain on 2026-05-14: minutes of dial timeouts and routing thrash, leaking into operator-visible queue stalls.

A quota-recovery probe loop exists at service_probe.go for providers marked `quota_exhausted` (periodic, with retry-after backoff). It does NOT cover `provider_connectivity` failures — those have no proactive recovery path.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/routehealth/store.go owns failure tracking; it is in-memory only, with no persistence interface (verified by file listing: only store.go / lease_store.go / utilization_store.go — no persist.go).
- service_probe.go's QuotaRecoveryProber only handles ProviderQuotaStateStore — not RouteHealth failures.
- No service-startup probe for configured providers. Providers in fizeau config are trusted to be reachable until a request proves otherwise.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup aliveness probe to internal/provider/openai/reachability.go (or a new internal/routehealth/probe.go). For each configured provider with a non-cloud base_url, run a single TCP-connect probe (bounded ~2s) on Service.New(). Record the result in routehealth.Store via a new RecordProbe(key, success, observedAt) method.
2. Add a periodic background probe loop (mirroring runQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop in service_probe.go): re-probe providers whose last probe is older than HealthProbeInterval (default 60s). Bound to local-ish providers — skip the cloud subscription routes that handle their own health.
3. Make routehealth.Store optionally persistent. ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth path (already proposed in fizeau-7cd66e6b) writes the probe + failure cache to a JSON snapshot with HealthSignalTTL (default 10 min). Subsequent processes read the snapshot at startup so a fresh ddx try doesn't re-discover bragi-is-dead.
4. Extend the routing gate: a candidate whose endpoint has a Probe.Success=false within HealthSignalTTL is FILTERED (not just score-demoted) when alternates exist. New FilterReason: endpoint_unreachable.
5. RouteDecision evidence: include the probe state (last_probe_at, last_probe_success) for each candidate so operators can audit why a candidate was filtered.

NON-SCOPE
- Don't probe cloud subscription routes (claude, openrouter, etc.) — those have their own health story via quota probing.
- Don't change the connection-failure retry policy (fizeau-8134d6db already shipped: 1 attempt, fail fast).
- Don't introduce a daemon — the periodic probe runs inside Service goroutines, same lifetime as quota-recovery loop.
- Don't probe at request time — request-time dial is the existing reactive path; this bead adds proactive probing on a separate schedule.

DEPS
- Composes with fizeau-7cd66e6b (persist route-health across processes) — same persistence layer.
- Composes with fizeau-361f4f4b (router reliability epic) — provides the missing aliveness signal that epic's AC #1 ("fall through to subscription within one decision") depends on.","events_attachment":"fizeau-f79d434b/events.jsonl","id":"fizeau-f79d434b","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:routing","area:routehealth","kind:feature","spec:endpoint-first-routing"],"owner":"erik","parent":"fizeau-361f4f4b","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7994fa4","spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"routing: proactive endpoint aliveness probing and durable cache for local providers","updated_at":"2026-05-15T00:57:27.56459079Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T00:19:34.836523403Z"}

  Public service surface (service*.go, routing_errors.go, internal/serviceimpl, internal/core) exposes Profile naming, ModelRef fields, ResolveProfile/ProfileAliases methods, and a sprawling FizeauService interface (17 methods). The v0.11 surface trims this to ~14 methods, renames Profile→Policy, drops ModelRef entirely, and removes target-language from comments/error strings.

ROOT CAUSE
  Public surface predates v0.11. Profile/ModelRef were the canonical inputs; ResolveProfile returned per-surface preview data tied to the dropped Surface concept. Target language is sprinkled through service_models.go and other files as legacy of the dropped target abstraction.

DESIGN INTENT (v0.11)
  - FizeauService interface methods: rename ListProfiles→ListPolicies; drop ResolveProfile and ProfileAliases entirely.
  - Types: ProfileInfo→PolicyInfo (no AliasOf field, no Target field); drop ResolvedProfile, ProfileSurface entirely.
  - Request fields: Profile→Policy on RouteRequest and ServiceExecuteRequest. Add AllowLocal bool, Require []string. Drop ModelRef from RouteRequest, ServiceExecuteRequest, and ResolvedModelRef field. Keep MinPower/MaxPower as soft signals.
  - RouteDecision.RequestedProfile→RequestedPolicy. RoutePowerPolicy{Profile, MinPower, MaxPower}→RoutePowerPolicy{PolicyName, MinPower, MaxPower}.
  - Public errors: ErrProfilePinConflict/ErrNoProfileCandidate/ErrUnknownProfile→ErrPolicyRequirementUnsatisfied + ErrUnknownPolicy + ErrUnsatisfiablePin (mirroring Phase 1 routing errors).
  - HarnessInfo: drop IsLocal/IsSubscription bools, replace with Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ProviderInfo: add IncludeByDefault bool, Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel.
  - ModelInfo: add Billing modelcatalog.BillingModel; drop CatalogRef field.
  - Scrub all routing-context "target" language from production code (comments, error strings, helper function names like catalogRefForModel). Add scoped lint rule preventing reintroduction in service_*.go, internal/routing/, internal/modelcatalog/.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
  - service.go:22-43 FizeauService interface (17 methods)
  - service.go:228-249 HarnessInfo (IsLocal/IsSubscription)
  - service.go:260-313 ProviderInfo, ModelInfo
  - service.go:321-360 ProfileInfo, ResolvedProfile, ProfileSurface
  - service.go:368-404 RouteRequest (Profile, ModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower fields)
  - service.go:441-484 RouteDecision, RoutePowerPolicy
  - service.go:676-796 ServiceExecuteRequest (Profile, ModelRef, ResolvedModelRef, MinPower/MaxPower)
  - service_profiles.go (~190 lines, rename to service_policies.go and rewrite)
  - service_routing.go (59+ Profile/ModelRef hits)
  - service_models.go:491,559 catalogRefForModel + target-level pricing comments
  - service_session_log.go:79-80,130-131 RequestedModelRef/ResolvedModelRef field assignments
  - service_override.go:108 ModelRef field assignment
  - routing_errors.go:170-251 ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate, ErrUnknownProfile

PROPOSED FIX
  1. service.go: rename interface methods (ListProfiles→ListPolicies; drop ResolveProfile, ProfileAliases). Replace ProfileInfo/ResolvedProfile/ProfileSurface with PolicyInfo. Update all request/response types (RouteRequest, RouteDecision, RoutePowerPolicy, ServiceExecuteRequest, HarnessInfo, ProviderInfo, ModelInfo) per design intent.
  2. Rename service_profiles.go → service_policies.go. Rewrite: implement ListPolicies returning []PolicyInfo from cat.Policies(). Drop everything else.
  3. service_routing.go: rename req.Profile→req.Policy; drop ModelRef reads; map cat.Policy(req.Policy) → routing.Request{Policy, AllowLocal, Require, MinPower, MaxPower}; rewrite escalateProfileLadder→escalatePolicyLadder; rewrite serviceRoutingReasoningResolver to query cat.LookupModel(modelID).ReasoningDefault directly (no surfaces).
  4. service_models.go: drop catalogRefForModel function (dead after ModelRef removal); rewrite cost fallback to use cat.LookupModel(modelID).CostInputPerMTok directly (no PricingFor target-level lookup).
  5. service_session_log.go: drop RequestedModelRef/ResolvedModelRef field assignments.
  6. service_override.go: drop ModelRef passthrough.
  7. routing_errors.go: replace public error types per design intent. Rename ErrNoLiveProvider.StartingTier→StartingPolicy.
  8. Scrub target-language: comments referring to "target" in routing context → "policy" or "model"; HealthTarget stays (different concept).
  9. Add scoped lint: new file internal/lint/targetlint/analyzer.go (modeled on DDx's routinglint) that flags identifiers and comments containing "target" in routing context inside service_*.go, internal/routing/, internal/modelcatalog/.
  10. Update all service_*_test.go inline YAML fixtures and struct field references (~25 files).

NON-SCOPE
  - Internal core (internal/core/) — Phase 7.
  - CLI flags — Phase 6.
  - Provider config (user-config) — Phase 5.
  - DDx coordination — Phase 9.

The Fizeau repo has 103 .go files at the repo root, all in `package fizeau` (or `package fizeau_test`). This includes service.go, service_routing.go, service_execute*.go, harness_golden_integration_test.go, contract003_*_test.go, metadata_billing.go, options_*.go, provider_*.go, public_api*.go, role_correlation.go, routing_errors*.go, and ~85 other files spanning the entire service surface.

This violates standard Go project layout:
- Library code lives under `internal/` or `pkg/`, not at the repo root.
- Root is typically reserved for CHANGELOG, README, LICENSE, go.mod, and (optionally) the `main` package binary.
- 103 files in one package is hard to navigate; package-level cohesion is lost.

It also creates spurious friction:
- IDE / `gopls` slows on the big package.
- Adding a new top-level helper requires choosing between "another root .go file" and "carve out a subpackage" with no precedent.
- The default ddx execution worktree clones the entire repo including the root .go pile, multiplying disk during the parallel-drain.

ROOT CAUSE
Historical accident. The Fizeau repo grew from a single-file prototype; new files kept landing at the root because that's where the existing service.go lived. There was never an explicit decision to move to internal/.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create `internal/service/` (or `internal/fizeau/` — choose based on import-name ergonomics).
2. Move all 103 root .go files into the new directory.
3. Rename their package declarations from `package fizeau` / `package fizeau_test` to `package service` / `package service_test` (or the chosen name).
4. Update all imports across the repo (internal/serviceimpl, cmd/fiz, cmd/fizeau, etc.) to reference the new path.
5. Add a thin `service.go` at the root if a public top-level entry point is still needed (or rely on cmd/).
6. Update CONTRACT-003 cross-references that point at root-level types (they're already documented by their type names; just file paths change).

NON-SCOPE
- Don't change the public API surface in CONTRACT-003. Type names and method signatures stay.
- Don't change the test surface — test names + assertions stay.
- Don't reshape the internal/ tree (internal/harnesses, internal/routing, etc. are fine where they are).
- Don't touch internal/serviceimpl until step 5 of the consuming refactor: that package is currently the only place service code is allowed to import per the harness-interface-refactor plan.

DEPS
- Don't start until the harness-interface refactor (plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md) drain completes. The refactor's per-harness migration touches service*.go consumers; doing both in parallel would create constant merge conflicts. Block on the lint rule landing (BEAD-HARNESS-IF-10 / fizeau-1e5bcee1) since that's the final harness-refactor step.
- Coordinate the auto-merge of any other in-flight beads that touch root-level files.

RISK
- Big-diff bead. Every consumer file changes (imports). Tests have to compile and pass against the new path. Recommend a single sweeping PR with `git mv` to preserve blame.
- Use `go build ./...` + `go test ./... -count=1` as the verification command; if either is red, the rewrite isn't done.","id":"fizeau-cd9af16b","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:repo-structure","kind:refactor"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"repo: move 103 root-level package fizeau files into internal/service/ for standard Go layout","updated_at":"2026-05-15T07:21:35.890181064Z"}

The `ProviderConfig.IncludeByDefault *bool` field at `internal/config/config.go` is plumbed into service config, but the reopened review found the existing routing bypass only covered provider/harness pins. Exact `Model` pins must also override provider `include_by_default: false`; otherwise an operator can request a known model and still have its provider removed from eligibility.

CURRENT STATE
The original implementation landed in commit `cc2fadf187a77b4bcc78480469acc60a68646df6`, then this bead was reopened with note: "include_by_default bypass only checks Provider/Harness pins, but FEAT/CONTRACT hard pins include exact Model. Add model-only pin behavior and tests."

PROPOSED FIX
In the routing eligibility path, keep excluding `IncludeByDefault: false` providers from unpinned/default routing, but treat any explicit hard pin on `Harness`, `Provider`, or exact `Model` as intentional and allow that provider to remain eligible when the pin resolves to it. Preserve route-status evidence for default-route exclusions.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `internal/routing/` eligibility/filter code and tests
- `internal/config/` tests only if additional config fixture coverage is needed
- `agentcli/` route-status tests only if surfaced output changes

NON-SCOPE
- Catalog-level `IncludeByDefault` defaults
- Quota-aware routing
- Per-model `ExactPinOnly` behavior unrelated to provider default inclusion","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint 2. `go test ./cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/...` passes (table tests verify the
   verdict logic against recorded fixtures).
3. Running `./fizeau-probe-reasoning --profile
   scripts/benchmark/profiles/fiz-openrouter-qwen3-6-27b.yaml` against the
   live OpenRouter endpoint produces a markdown table with one row per matrix
   point, a verdict line, and exit code 0.
4. The artifact directory contains the raw request and response bodies for
   each matrix point, named so the operator can inspect them without re-
   running the probe.
5. `make probe-reasoning PROFILE=<path>` runs the tool with a representative
   default matrix.","claimed-at":"2026-05-11T19:55:38Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"525134","closing_commit_sha":"17c5e7f0734c4d7564b5ed7d2794efb25f2d429c","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:52:27.26271318Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fizeau-d72b32a0","depends_on_id":"fizeau-e80c156e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T21:14:26Z"}],"description":"Build a CLI at cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning that snapshots provider
introspection AND verifies whether the upstream actually behaves the way
introspection claims. Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11, the probe is now
primarily a verification tool, not a discovery tool — introspection
adapters (fizeau-e80c156e) handle discovery where it's available.

Three modes of output per (provider, model):

1. **Snapshot** — verbatim capture of every introspection endpoint the
   provider exposes:
     ds4:           /props, /v1/models, /v1/models/<id>
     llama-server:  /props, /v1/models, /slots, /health, /metrics
     OR:            /api/v1/models, /api/v1/models/<id> if supported
     others:        whatever GET <base>/v1/models returns
   Written verbatim into the artifact directory as JSON files. This is
   the auditable record of \"what the server claimed on date X.\"

2. **Verification matrix** — send a small set of seeded chat completions
   that exercise each documented wire knob:
     - reasoning: off
     - reasoning: low (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=2048)
     - reasoning: medium (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=8192)
     - reasoning: high (KindNamed, PortableBudgets=32768)
     - reasoning: 4096 (KindTokens explicit)
     - reasoning: 16384 (KindTokens explicit)
   For each row capture: wire body sent, finish_reason,
   usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens (if present), len of
   message.reasoning_content, wall time. Hash of <think> block content
   when present (so flat-mapping is visible).

   Use a stable single-prompt with seed pinning when the upstream supports
   it (sampling.seed). Prompt: \"What is 17 times 24? Show your work step
   by step.\" Small enough to run fast (~30-60s per request on local
   lanes); large enough to produce measurable thinking.

3. **Verdict** — one of:
     introspection-trusted: claims and observations match (e.g. ds4's
       alias map predicts low/medium/xhigh produce identical thinking
       depth, and the matrix confirms within sampling noise).
     introspection-divergent: claims and observations disagree. Print
       the disagreement and flag for operator review.
     introspection-absent: no useful introspection endpoint; catalog L2
       required. Print recommended `reasoning_wire` value.

CLI surface:
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --profile <path> [--artifact-dir <path>]
                          [--json] [--snapshot-only]
                          [--matrix-only] [--seed <int>]
  fizeau-probe-reasoning --provider <type> --base-url <url> --model <id>
                          [--api-key-env <var>] ...

Default artifact-dir: /tmp/fizeau-probe-<timestamp>/<provider>/

Run timeout per request: 5 minutes (long-thinking Qwen3 / DS4 can take
60s+ on local hardware).

Makefile: add `make probe-reasoning PROFILE=<path>` target.

In-scope files:
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/main.go (new)
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/snapshot.go (new) — introspection endpoint
  capture per provider type.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/matrix.go (new) — verification matrix
  runner.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/verdict.go (new) — claim-vs-observation
  diff logic.
- cmd/fizeau-probe-reasoning/probe_test.go (new) — table tests against
  recorded fixtures (no live endpoint needed for unit tests).
- Makefile — `probe-reasoning` target.

Out-of-scope:
- Auto-writing back to the catalog (operator reviews and writes
  manually).
- Sweeping multiple models in one invocation (shell loop is fine).
- Real tokenizer for reasoning_content length conversion (use chars/4
  approximation, matching the telemetry bead fizeau-8f62bcbb).

2. TestScorePolicy_MinPowerSonnetBeatsOpus_DefaultPolicy: given two candidates (sonnet power=8 cost=0 subscription quota_ok; opus power=10 cost=0.045 subscription quota_ok), with MinPower=8, the sonnet candidate's total score is higher than opus.
3. TestScorePolicy_MaxPowerDoesNotDisableProviderPreference asserts that with MaxPower=8 set, a 'subscription-first' provider preference still adds the +30 quota_health bonus.
4. Existing service_routing_test.go tests (TestRouteWith* etc.) still pass — regression guard for non-power-bounded scoring.
5. go test ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-14T23:31:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2237932","closing_commit_sha":"8b7de9967773575bf37bed00eeb83d0dcc72a095","created_at":"2026-05-14T23:28:12.475336117Z","description":"PROBLEM
internal/routing/score.go:60 and :108 wrap the policy-aware scoring (cheap/default/smart preferences for local/subscription/cost) and the provider-preference bias in `if !hasPowerBounds`. When a caller sets --min-power N or --max-power M, those branches are skipped entirely. Result: among candidates that all satisfy the bounds, the routing falls through to raw base+quota+power-9-bonus scoring, which systematically prefers higher-power-metered models over equivalent-quality subscription-free models.

Observed today: ddx try --harness claude --min-power 8 routed to claude/opus (power 10, score ~76, metered ~$0.045/turn) instead of claude/claude-sonnet-4-6 (power 8, score 186 per fiz route-status without bounds, subscription/free). Across the 11 harness-interface refactor bead executions, this cost real opus tokens for work sonnet would have handled fine.

ROOT CAUSE
- score.go:52: hasPowerBounds := cand.MinPower > 0 || cand.MaxPower > 0
- score.go:60: `if !hasPowerBounds { switch policy ... }` — the entire policy preference block (deployment locality, quota health, cost-class ranking) is gated off
- score.go:108: `if !hasPowerBounds { switch cand.ProviderPreference ... }` — provider-preference bias gated off

The intent was probably: 'when user expresses explicit power bounds, just enforce bounds and ignore default biases.' But power bounds are orthogonal to policy preference — a user asking for power>=8 still wants the cheaper, subscription-backed candidate among the eligible set.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the `if !hasPowerBounds` guards at score.go:60 and score.go:108. Apply policy preference and provider-preference bias unconditionally to in-bounds candidates. Keep the below-MinPower / above-MaxPower penalties as they are (lines 227-238) — those correctly demote out-of-bounds candidates without disabling policy scoring for in-bounds ones.

The natural-power bonus at line 222 (`Power * 12` when no bounds set) can stay gated — that's a different signal: 'when no bounds expressed, treat higher power as a quality proxy.' When bounds ARE expressed, the bounds themselves encode the power preference; double-counting via natural-power-bonus would be wrong.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the below-MinPower / above-MaxPower penalty values (lines 227-238).
- Do not change the natural-power bonus gating at line 222.
- Do not change quota-pressure, cooldown, sticky-affinity, or perf scoring.
- Do not introduce new score components.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude","requested_min_power":8,"requested_max_power":8}","created_at":"2026-05-14T23:30:34.803688472Z","kind":"routing","source":"legacy agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"actual_harness":"claude","actual_model":"","actual_power":0,"actual_provider":"","attempt_id":"20260514T233033-4c343a6f","bead_id":"fizeau-dc3cf359","inferred_power_class":"standard","rejected_route_pins":null,"requested_profile":"","routing_intent_degraded":true,"routing_intent_note":"actual route facts unavailable","routing_intent_source":"heuristic","smart_justification":""}","created_at":"2026-05-14T23:30:34.886097714Z","kind":"execution-routing-intent","source":"ddx work","summary":"source=heuristic powerClass=standard harness=claude note=actual route facts unavailable"},{"actor":"erik","body":"{"base_rev":"a7dbbc5a9187b292ad7ef2ded5160c4348d43b4d","classification":"","decode_warnings":[{"field":"output","warning":"empty output"}],"detail":"agent: invalid power bounds: min_power=8 must be less than max_power=8","malformed":true,"rationale":"","recommended_action":"","result_rev":"a7dbbc5a9187b292ad7ef2ded5160c4348d43b4d","session_id":"eb-fb6b8715","status":"execution_failed","suggested_amendments":[],"suggested_followup_beads":[]}","created_at":"2026-05-14T23:31:06.577774703Z","kind":"bead-quality.triage-warning","source":"ddx work","summary":"empty output"},{"actor":"erik","body":"agent: invalid power bounds: min_power=8 must be less than max_power=8

`ResolveRoute` currently calls `buildRoutingInputsWithCatalog`, which enumerates harnesses/providers and probes provider models through service-specific helpers such as `liveProviderEntries`. SD-005 requires auto-routing to consume the same enriched available-model snapshot exposed by `fiz models`, then expand snapshot rows into dispatch candidates. The routing engine itself should be preserved; the input source should change.

CHANGE
Add a snapshot-to-`routing.Inputs` adapter and wire `ResolveRoute` to use it. The adapter must preserve hard pins (`Harness`, `Provider`, exact `Model`), IncludeByDefault/default-deny behavior, auto-routability/exact-pin-only behavior, context/tool/reasoning gates, quota/cooldown signals, endpoint utilization, and sticky server-instance affinity. Unknown snapshot models must remain exact-pinnable but excluded from unpinned automatic routing.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- service_routing.go
- internal/routing input tests if adapter logic belongs there
- service_routing_test.go / service_model_resolution_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Changing score component names.
- RouteStatus rendering.
- Root ListModels migration, which should already be complete before this bead The user-facing
deliverable is a `fiz models` CLI subcommand. The architectural
deliverable is the foundation for the auto-router: a runtime-resolved
view of "what can fizeau actually use right now," assembled from all
discovery sources, enriched with catalog metadata, surfaced through a
multi-process-safe disk cache.

## End goal

For a given fizeau install at a given time, there is a canonical set
of known models — one entry per (provider, model_id) — with defined
properties: identity, parsed family/version/tier, catalog-derived
power/cost/reasoning_levels/quota_pool, and runtime-derived status/
quota_remaining/recent latency. Operators introspect via `fiz models`;
routing reads the same snapshot to score candidates. Single source of
truth; no separate "what does routing think exists" vs "what does
operator see" divergence.

## Child beads (in dependency order)

- **M0** (ADR-012): per-source on-disk cache for discovery + runtime
  signals. Defines file layout, locking semantics, single-flight
  refresh algorithm, atomic-write contract, test matrix.
- **M1**: cache infrastructure — implements ADR-012's algorithm with
  the 10-test robustness matrix. Includes `ParseModelRef`/canonical
  identity helpers.
- **M2**: snapshot assembler — enumerate providers (incl harnesses-
  as-providers) → discover via cache → parse via tier-ranker
  (depends on routing bead A) → enrich from catalog (joins on
  power, cost, reasoning_levels, quota_pool from bead E1).
- **M3**: runtime signal collector — status, quota_remaining, recent
  p50 latency → runtime cache.
- **M4**: `fiz models` top-level CLI subcommand. Default filtered
  view; `--include-noise --refresh --no-refresh --json --provider X
  --power-min/max`. Detail view via `fiz models <ref>` with shortform
  fuzzy resolution and full-canonical exact match.
- **M5** (deferred): OR sub-provider expansion via `@<sub_provider>`
  syntax. Filed proposed-status; future work.

## Architecture invariants

1. **Identity = (provider, model_id).** Canonical form `<provider>/
   <model_id>`. Provider names slug-safe; model IDs preserved
   provider-side. Split-on-first-slash.
2. **Harness == provider** for snapshot purposes. Subscription harness
   wrappers participate as named providers (e.g.,
   `claude-subscription`, `codex-subscription`).
3. **Catalog is implementation detail.** No catalog-only entries
   appear in `fiz models`. If no provider serves a model, it's not
   "known."
4. **No aliases in snapshot.** Aliases are a search/discovery
   concern (CLI-side fuzzy resolution); snapshot stores canonical
   entries only.
5. **Per-source on-disk cache**, multi-process-safe, with strict
   100ms UI budget. Discovery cache long-TTL; runtime cache short-TTL.
6. **Single-flight refresh** across processes via PID+marker pattern
   (extending fizeau's existing `acquireMatrixLock` idiom).
7. **Stale-while-revalidate** by default. Force-refresh waits.
8. **Atomic write** via tmp+fsync+rename. No torn reads.

## Governing doc updates

- **NEW: ADR-012** — per-source disk cache for discovery + runtime
- **FEAT-006** (standalone CLI) — add `fiz models` subcommand
- **FEAT-004** (model routing) — addendum: routing reads snapshot
- **ADR-009** (routing surface redesign) — addendum: harness-as-
  provider in snapshot

## Closes when

All five active children (M0, M1, M2, M3, M4) closed. M5 is filed

Introspection adapters read provider /props at construction time and
override the static ThinkingFormat default with what the live server
actually claims.

Per ADR-010 Amendment 2026-05-11 the L1 layer (live introspection) is the
authoritative source where available; this bead implements L1 for ds4 and
llama-server. The static defaults that fizeau-073fe18b sets become the L3
floor; L2 (catalog reasoning_wire) sits in between.

After this bead lands, fizeau auto-detects:
- ds4's alias map → effective reasoning_levels without catalog curation
- llama-server's chat_template variant → auto-detect kwargs vs top-level
  envelope (today's wire choice is hardcoded by fizeau-073fe18b)
- server-side reasoning_format default → can override per-request only
  when needed

Cost model (measured 2026-05-11):
  ds4 /props on LAN:         p50=13ms  max=51ms  (n=10)
  sindri /props on LAN:      p50=9ms   max=13ms  (n=10)
  OR /api/v1/models cloud:   p50=316ms max=325ms (n=5)

Three adapters in this bead:

1. **ds4 adapter** (internal/provider/ds4/introspection.go, new):
   GET http://<base>/props. Parse reasoning.{supported_efforts, aliases,
   default, effective_default, think_max_min_context},
   api.supported_request_parameters, sampling, runtime. Derive effective
   ThinkingFormat=OpenAIEffort and effective reasoning_levels from
   supported_efforts minus aliased duplicates.

2. **llama-server adapter** (internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection.go,
   new): GET http://<base>/props. Parse
   default_generation_settings.params.reasoning_format, chat_template
   (substring match for `enable_thinking` to detect kwargs envelope),
   chat_template_caps.supports_preserve_reasoning, build_info.

3. **Common plumbing** (internal/provider/registry/introspection.go, new):
   ProviderIntrospection struct, registry of adapters by provider type,
   cache by (base_url, model), non-fatal failure with structured warning.

Construction order: instantiate provider → run introspection adapter (if
registered) → merge into capabilities → ready. Per-request path is
unchanged; no introspection IO at request time.

In-scope files:
- internal/provider/ds4/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/ds4/introspection_test.go (new)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/introspection_test.go (new)
- internal/provider/registry/introspection.go (new)
- internal/provider/registry/registry.go (wire adapter invocation)
- internal/provider/ds4/ds4.go (consume introspection)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/llamaserver.go (consume introspection)
- internal/provider/ds4/testdata/props/ (recorded fixtures)
- internal/provider/llamaserver/testdata/props/ (recorded fixtures)

Out-of-scope (separate beads):
- OR adapter (introspection thin; catalog L2 covers it).
- Anthropic-native / OpenAI-native adapters.
- vLLM adapter (defer until lane re-added).

New fixture testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json + test case asserts classification as invalid_provider. 2. scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run on existing history flags the 54+ known no-response cells transitioning from '' to 'invalid_provider'. 3. Apply pass leaves those cells tagged and excluded from pass@k denominator. 4. A subsequent sweep with --retry-invalid re-runs them.","created_at":"2026-05-11T15:30:29.841306558Z","description":"Currently when fiz makes an llm.request and gets no llm.response back (cloud-side hang, 5xx, stream-aborted, rate-limit drop, etc.), the cell ends up as a normal graded_fail with reward=0, turns=0, terminated_mid_work=None. The matrix runner treats it identically to a real model failure — even though no actual model attempt happened. Result: 54 of OR-Qwen's 239 "failures" (22%) on TB-2.1 are this class, polluting the pass-rate denominator and wasting the cell entirely.

Two complementary fixes:

A. **Matrix-side classifier rule** (low-effort, high-impact):
   In cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go, add: if final_status="graded_fail" AND
   (turns is nil OR turns==0) AND terminated_mid_work is nil AND (input_tokens
   is nil OR input_tokens==0), classify as `invalid_provider`. The existing
   `--retry-invalid` flag will then re-run these on the next sweep pass.
   Mirrors the same structural-rule pattern as the recent harness_crash →
   invalid_setup and ran-no-attempt → invalid_setup additions.

B. **In-fiz request-level retry** (deeper, may belong in a separate bead):
   When fiz issues an llm.request and gets no streamed tokens within
   provider_timeout, retry with exponential backoff (e.g. 1s, 4s, 16s)
   up to 3 attempts before failing the turn. Should be capped by total
   wall budget. This stops the request from ever reaching "no llm.response"
   state in the first place. Requires care to avoid amplifying real
   server-side stalls into runaway retries.

Acceptance is a command:

1. With a stub provider that returns HTTP 200 with empty body (simulating
   the no-response state), `go test ./cmd/bench/... -run TestClassifyMatrixInvalid`
   has a new fixture report (`no-llm-response.json`) with the structural
   shape above, asserted to classify as `invalid_provider`.
2. `scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py --dry-run` on the existing
   3700+ historical reports flags the 54 known OR-Qwen no-response cells
   plus any other lanes' equivalents as transitioning from `""` to
   `invalid_provider`. After apply, those cells are excluded from the
   pass@k denominator and tagged for `--retry-invalid` reruns.
3. (Optional, defer to Part B bead) `internal/core/loop_test.go` covers
   the retry-on-empty-response behavior with a stub provider that yields
   nothing on the first 2 attempts and a normal response on the 3rd.

In-scope files for Part A (this bead):
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid.go — add the classifier branch
- cmd/bench/matrix_invalid_test.go — add fixture test
- cmd/bench/testdata/matrix-invalid/no-llm-response.json — new fixture
- scripts/benchmark/reclassify-invalid.py — mirror the Go logic
- scripts/benchmark/backfill-terminated-mid-work.py (already handles
  this case via the structural rule but not the classifier tag)

Out-of-scope:
- Part B (in-fiz request retry) — file as a separate bead if/when needed.
- Distinguishing "5xx" vs "rate-limit" vs "timeout" — at the report.json
  level we just see "no llm.response"; downstream telemetry could refine
  but this bead just gets us to "retriable cell".
- Adjusting harbor's own retry behavior.

Why this matters for the QWEN_BASE experiment:
The OR-Qwen 2.5x rerun experiment (in flight as of 2026-05-11 15:29Z) will
re-run the 239 previously-failed cells. If 22% of those originally failed
with "no llm.response", we expect roughly the same fraction to hit the
same class on the rerun. This bead would catch them with `--retry-invalid`
on a subsequent pass instead of leaving them as silent failures.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint hook: canceled: context canceled"}","created_at":"2026-05-11T15:31:43.017626355Z","kind":"bead-quality.lint","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"warning score=0"},{"actor":"erik","body":"{"detail":"pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging checkpoint changes: fatal: unable to stat '.ddx/.cleanup.lock/acquired_at': No such file or directory: exit status 128","harness":"","model":"","reason":"context_canceled"}","created_at":"2026-05-11T15:31:44.782598664Z","kind":"disruption_detected","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"context_canceled"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"actual_harness":"","actual_model":"","actual_power":0,"actual_provider":"","attempt_id":"","bead_id":"fizeau-e874633d","rejected_route_pins":null,"requested_tier":"standard","routing_intent_degraded":true,"routing_intent_note":"actual route facts unavailable","routing_intent_source":"heuristic","smart_justification":""}","created_at":"2026-05-11T15:31:44.789724435Z","kind":"execution-routing-intent","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"source=heuristic tier=standard note=actual route facts unavailable"},{"actor":"erik","body":"pre-execute-bead checkpoint: staging checkpoint changes: fatal: unable to stat '.ddx/.cleanup.lock/acquired_at': No such file or directory: exit status 128","created_at":"2026-05-11T15:31:44.802812021Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"}],"id":"fizeau-e874633d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:benchmark","kind:reliability","kind:classifier"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"bench: tag 'no llm.response' cells as invalid_provider so --retry-invalid catches them","updated_at":"2026-05-11T15:39:22.114864631Z"}

+{"acceptance":"1. TestServiceStartup_ProbesConfiguredProviders asserts that Service.New() runs one TCP-connect probe per configured non-cloud provider within startup-bounded time (e.g. 5s total cap regardless of provider count).
2. TestProbeLoop_RetriesDeadProvidersOnInterval asserts that a provider marked unreachable by probe is re-probed every HealthProbeInterval and recorded back to reachable when it comes online.
3. TestRouter_FiltersUnreachableEndpointsWhenAlternatesExist asserts that when bragi:1234 is recorded unreachable AND claude/sonnet is a valid candidate, the router emits exactly one routing decision selecting claude/sonnet without first dialing bragi.
4. TestRouter_AcceptsUnreachableEndpointWhenSoleCandidate asserts that when the only configured candidate is the unreachable one (explicit operator pin), the router still attempts the request and surfaces the dial failure honestly.
5. TestRouteHealth_PersistsProbeResults — paired with fizeau-7cd66e6b's persistence story, asserts a fresh process reads the prior probe results within HealthSignalTTL.
6. RouteDecision evidence includes per-candidate {last_probe_at, last_probe_success} fields where applicable.
7. CHANGELOG.md notes the aliveness probing + new ServiceOptions knobs (HealthProbeInterval, HealthSignalTTL).
8. go test ./internal/routehealth/... ./internal/routing/... ./... -count=1 passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T00:19:34Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2417331","closing_commit_sha":"443c939d3d38e79260e714d8f0b8c0d75d5f8d9b","created_at":"2026-05-15T00:14:16.355963324Z","description":"PROBLEM
Fizeau has no proactive aliveness check for configured providers. A dead local provider (e.g. bragi:1234, grendel:1234) is only learned to be dead by attempting a request against it and eating the dial timeout. The failure feedback is stored in internal/routehealth.Store, which is:
1. Process-local (sync.RWMutex map; not persisted to disk).
2. Short-TTL (DefaultCooldown = 30s; entries expire and are re-tried).

Result for an operator with bragi unplugged but configured:
- Every fresh `ddx work` or `ddx try` process re-probes bragi on its first routing decision, eating a 5s dial timeout (post fizeau-b5757624) or 30s pre-fix.
- Even within a long-running `ddx work --watch`, the route exclusion expires every 30s and bragi gets re-attempted on the next cycle.
- Aggregate cost over a 27-bead drain on 2026-05-14: minutes of dial timeouts and routing thrash, leaking into operator-visible queue stalls.

A quota-recovery probe loop exists at service_probe.go for providers marked `quota_exhausted` (periodic, with retry-after backoff). It does NOT cover `provider_connectivity` failures — those have no proactive recovery path.

ROOT CAUSE
- internal/routehealth/store.go owns failure tracking; it is in-memory only, with no persistence interface (verified by file listing: only store.go / lease_store.go / utilization_store.go — no persist.go).
- service_probe.go's QuotaRecoveryProber only handles ProviderQuotaStateStore — not RouteHealth failures.
- No service-startup probe for configured providers. Providers in fizeau config are trusted to be reachable until a request proves otherwise.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup aliveness probe to internal/provider/openai/reachability.go (or a new internal/routehealth/probe.go). For each configured provider with a non-cloud base_url, run a single TCP-connect probe (bounded ~2s) on Service.New(). Record the result in routehealth.Store via a new RecordProbe(key, success, observedAt) method.
2. Add a periodic background probe loop (mirroring runQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop in service_probe.go): re-probe providers whose last probe is older than HealthProbeInterval (default 60s). Bound to local-ish providers — skip the cloud subscription routes that handle their own health.
3. Make routehealth.Store optionally persistent. ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth path (already proposed in fizeau-7cd66e6b) writes the probe + failure cache to a JSON snapshot with HealthSignalTTL (default 10 min). Subsequent processes read the snapshot at startup so a fresh ddx try doesn't re-discover bragi-is-dead.
4. Extend the routing gate: a candidate whose endpoint has a Probe.Success=false within HealthSignalTTL is FILTERED (not just score-demoted) when alternates exist. New FilterReason: endpoint_unreachable.
5. RouteDecision evidence: include the probe state (last_probe_at, last_probe_success) for each candidate so operators can audit why a candidate was filtered.

NON-SCOPE
- Don't probe cloud subscription routes (claude, openrouter, etc.) — those have their own health story via quota probing.
- Don't change the connection-failure retry policy (fizeau-8134d6db already shipped: 1 attempt, fail fast).
- Don't introduce a daemon — the periodic probe runs inside Service goroutines, same lifetime as quota-recovery loop.
- Don't probe at request time — request-time dial is the existing reactive path; this bead adds proactive probing on a separate schedule.

DEPS
- Composes with fizeau-7cd66e6b (persist route-health across processes) — same persistence layer.
- Composes with fizeau-361f4f4b (router reliability epic) — provides the missing aliveness signal that epic's AC #1 ("fall through to subscription within one decision") depends The recovery loop is implemented as pure functions (`runQuotaRecoveryProbePass`/`runQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop`) with explicit `now`/`sleep` seams, enabling deterministic tests. The new `AllExhausted` helper correctly returns elapsed entries that `ExhaustedAt` filters out — necessary for the probe loop to find work. Backoff bookkeeping is cleaned up when providers leave the exhausted set, avoiding stale state across re-exhaustion cycles.

data.output:
ID:       fizeau-cd9af16b
Title:    repo: move 103 root-level package fizeau files into internal/service/ for standard Go layout
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:repo-structure, kind:refactor
Deps:     fizeau-1e5bcee1
Desc:     PROBLEM
The Fizeau repo has 103 .go files at the repo root, all in `package fizeau` (or `package fizeau_test`). This includes service.go, service_routing.go, service_execute*.go, harness_golden_integration_test.go, contract003_*_test.go, metadata_billing.go, options_*.go, provider_*.go, public_api*.go, role_correlation.go, routing_errors*.go, and ~85 other files spanning the entire service surface.

This violates standard Go project layout:
- Library code lives under `internal/` or `pkg/`, not at the repo root.
- Root is typically reserved for CHANGELOG, README, LICENSE, go.mod, and (optionally) the `main` package binary.
- 103 files in one package is hard to navigate; package-level cohesion is lost.

It also creates spurious friction:
- IDE / `gopls` slows on the big package.
- Adding a new top-level helper requires choosing between "another root .go file" and "carve out a subpackage" with no precedent.
- The default ddx execution worktree clones the entire repo including the root .go pile, multiplying disk during the parallel-drain.


ID:       fizeau-0247682e
Title:    repo-structure: reconcile root-package move with ADR-008 and inventory facade vs implementation files
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:design, area:repo-structure, kind:refactor, spec:ADR-008
Parent:   fizeau-cd9af16b
Desc:     PROBLEM
The parent bead `fizeau-cd9af16b` proposes moving every root-level `package fizeau` file into `internal/service/` and requires zero `.go` files at the module root. Repository evidence conflicts with that plan:
- ADR-008 says the root `package fizeau` is the public facade at `github.com/easel/fizeau` and that the module import path must stay stable.
- The generated embedding docs document the root package as the importable library surface.
- agentcli/, cmd/bench/, cmd/fiz/, and root black-box tests import `github.com/easel/fizeau` directly.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-008-service-package-and-transcript-boundaries.md:43-64` explicitly keeps the root package as the compatibility boundary and moves only concrete implementation code behind `internal/`. `service.go:18-20` defines the root `FizeauService` public interface. `website/content/docs/embedding/_index.md:949-974` documents `github.com/easel/fizeau` as the root facade and lists importable public packages. Therefore parent AC #1 (zero root `.go` files) is not executable without either violating ADR-008 or first designing a replacement public facade.

PROPOSED FIX
Write a cold-readable design/inventory doc under `docs/helix/02-design/` that converts the oversized parent into an ordered extraction plan. The doc must inventory root-level `.go` files, bucket each as public facade, public compatibility alias/shim, or implementation-owned file, list in-repo consumers of `github.com/easel/fizeau`, and name follow-on extraction slices with target internal package owners.

data.text:
Decomposed `fizeau-cd9af16b` instead of attempting the move.

Child beads created in DDx:
- `fizeau-0247682e` — reconcile the root-package move with ADR-008 and inventory facade vs implementation files
- `fizeau-3d17862f` — extract execution, transcript, and session-log implementation from the root facade
- `fizeau-930b498b` — extract routing, quota, and status implementation from the root facade
- `fizeau-a2a8a45a` — extract model/provider listing helpers, refresh docs, and finish the root-facade audit

I reopened/unclaimed the parent bead and added a decomposition note. The rationale is in [no_changes_rationale.txt](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-cd9af16b-20260515T071411-d32ddd99/.ddx/executions/20260515T071411-d32ddd99/no_changes_rationale.txt).

Why it was split:
- The bead is too large by scope alone: 8 ACs across design, code motion, imports, docs, changelog, and full-repo verification.
- Repo evidence also shows a design conflict: ADR-008 keeps `github.com/easel/fizeau` as the public root facade, so the parent AC requiring zero root `.go` files is not executable as written.

I did not make a commit. No production code changed, and the only available tracker export path rewrote unrelated `.ddx/beads.jsonl` history, so I left the decomposition in the DDx live tracker plus the execution rationale instead. I did not run project tests.
